compatibleScales.relationship: Primary major scale of the key center
compatibleScales.rationale: The fundamental major scale built on C# as the tonic. Contains all seven diatonic notes that define C# major harmony and establishes the key center
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal major tonality; essential for classical, pop, rock, and all Western music. The foundation for understanding all other scales in this key
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use as the primary scale for C# major compositions and improvisations. Essential for establishing strong tonal center and major key feeling
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Folk, Blues

compatibleScales.relationship: Primary minor scale of the key center
compatibleScales.rationale: The fundamental natural minor scale built on C# as the tonic. Relative major is E major, sharing the same key signature but with C# as the tonal center
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal minor tonality; works across all genres from classical to rock, metal, and pop. Creates melancholic, introspective, or dark emotional character
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary choice for C# minor compositions. Use over Cm chord progressions, especially i-iv-v. Essential for natural minor harmony
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Blues, Gothic, Grunge

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - scale tones 1, 2, 3, 5, 6 only
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains only the most consonant notes of C# major (C#, D#, E#, G#, A#), avoiding the 4th and 7th which create tension. Completely safe for any C# major harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres; creates bright, optimistic melodies. Especially effective in rock, blues, country, folk, and pop music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for improvisation with guaranteed consonance. Works over I-IV-V progressions in C# major. Ideal for creating singable melodies and memorable hooks
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Blues, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic built on tonic
compatibleScales.rationale: The most universal minor scale containing C#, E, F#, G#, B. Avoids the 2nd and 6th scale degrees, providing pure minor character without dissonance
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Essential for rock, blues, metal, and funk. The foundation of blues-rock guitar solos and the most commonly used scale in rock music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Go-to scale for C# minor improvisation. Works perfectly over Cm chord vamps and i-iv-v progressions. Universal minor sound that never clashes
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Funk, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic with added b5 blue note
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds the characteristic blue note (G natural) to the minor pentatonic, creating the definitive blues/rock sound. The b5 creates tension that resolves to the perfect 5th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Essential for blues, rock, jazz-blues fusion, and funk. The signature sound of blues guitar from B.B. King to Stevie Ray Vaughan
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# dominant 7th or minor 7th chords. The G natural blue note is the key tension note - use it as a passing tone or bend into it
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Funk, Jazz Blues

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor mode with major 6th (A#)
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with raised 6th degree, creating a sophisticated minor sound that's brighter than Aeolian. The major 6th provides a jazz/funk characteristic
compatibleScales.musicalContext: The go-to minor scale for jazz, funk, fusion, and contemporary styles. More versatile than natural minor due to the major 6th. Used extensively by Miles Davis and Herbie Hancock
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary choice for modern minor improvisation. Works beautifully over C#m7 and F#m7 chords. Perfect for modal vamps and ii-V progressions in minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Fusion, R&B, Soul

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural minor with raised 7th degree
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with major 7th (B#), creating the characteristic augmented 2nd interval between A and B#. Provides strong V7-i resolution in minor keys
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical music, neoclassical metal, flamenco, Middle Eastern music. The signature minor scale of Bach and Yngwie Malmsteen
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for classical minor harmony and V7-i cadences. Use over C#m with G#7 dominant chords. The B# leading tone creates strong pull to tonic
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Metal, Neoclassical, Flamenco, Tango

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural minor with raised 6th and 7th degrees
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor scale with both A# and B# (major 6th and 7th), creating a smooth melodic line. More consonant than harmonic minor while maintaining minor character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, modern classical. The foundation of jazz minor harmony and the source of altered dominant scales. Essential for contemporary improvisation
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary jazz minor scale. Use ascending over C#m(maj7) chords. Opens up the entire melodic minor modal system for advanced improvisation
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Major scale with b7 - dominant 7th character
compatibleScales.rationale: Major scale with B natural instead of B#, creating the dominant 7th chord sound. The b7 provides bluesy, funky, or rock character to the major scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk, folk; dominant 7th chord vamps. Signature sound of The Beatles, Grateful Dead, Allman Brothers. Essential for Southern rock and jam bands
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over C#7 chords and I7 vamps. Use for blues-rock solos with major tonality. The B natural is the characteristic note to emphasize
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Country, Southern Rock, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: Major scale with raised 4th degree (#4)
compatibleScales.rationale: C# major with F## (double sharp) instead of F#, creating a bright, ethereal, dreamlike quality. The #4 creates characteristic Lydian color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores, progressive rock, fusion. The signature sound of Joe Satriani, Steve Vai, and John Williams film scores. Creates uplifting, floating feeling
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#maj7 or C#maj7#11 chords for modal compositions. Perfect for I chord vamps and impressionistic harmony. Emphasize the F## for Lydian character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor scale with b2 - Spanish/flamenco character
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with D natural instead of D#, creating the characteristic flamenco/Spanish sound. The b2 creates exotic, dark, Spanish character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, progressive rock, Middle Eastern music. Essential for Phrygian dominant sound in metal (Metallica, Testament). Creates dark, mysterious atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m or C#sus chords for Spanish or metal flavors. Emphasize the D natural for Phrygian character. Perfect for riffs in E Phrygian dominant style
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Pop, Rock, Singer-Songwriter, Ballad

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative major - shares all notes with C# minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E major is the relative major of C# minor, containing the exact same notes but centered on E. This relationship is fundamental to understanding major/minor duality
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres. Understanding relative relationships is essential for modulation and understanding how major and minor keys relate
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in E major when playing over C# minor progressions. The vi-I relationship opens up harmonic possibilities. Perfect for major sections in minor key compositions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Metal, Alternative, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale in E major system - subdominant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Dorian is the second mode of E major (relative major of C# minor). Provides subdominant minor function with sophisticated jazzy character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, funk, modal rock. Perfect for creating modal interplay between C# and F# minor centers. Common in Santana and Miles Davis style playing
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#m7 chords in C# minor contexts. Creates smooth modal movement between tonic and subdominant. Excellent for ii-i progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Blues, Rock, Funk, Country

compatibleScales.relationship: iii modal scale in E major - mediant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Phrygian shares all notes with C# natural minor and E major. Provides exotic color when used over G# minor chords in the key
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, flamenco-influenced rock, progressive metal. Creates tension and exotic flavor when emphasizing the A natural b2 of G# Phrygian
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#m chords for Phrygian color. Creates interesting modal contrast with C# minor. Perfect for iii-i progressions with Spanish flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Hard Rock, Grunge

compatibleScales.relationship: vii° modal scale - diminished function
compatibleScales.rationale: A# Locrian contains all notes of C# minor/E major but centers on the diminished vii° chord. Creates tension and instability that wants to resolve
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Advanced jazz, progressive rock, modern classical. Rarely used as a primary scale but valuable for creating tension over diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use briefly over A#dim or A#m7b5 chords for tension that resolves to C#m. Most effective as a passing sonority rather than tonal center
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Southern Rock, Jazz Blues

compatibleScales.relationship: 5th mode of harmonic minor - exotic dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Combines Phrygian b2 with major 3rd, creating Spanish/flamenco dominant character. Contains both D natural (b2) and E# (major 3rd) for exotic tension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, Middle Eastern music, film scores. Signature sound of flamenco guitar and Phrygian-dominant metal riffs. Creates dramatic, exotic atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 chords with Spanish or exotic flavor. Essential for flamenco-style cadences. The D-E# relationship creates characteristic tension
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: 4th mode of melodic minor - altered major sound
compatibleScales.rationale: Combines Lydian #4 with Mixolydian b7, creating sophisticated dominant color. Contains F## and B for jazz-fusion dominant character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, modern jazz, progressive rock. Signature scale of jazz fusion guitarists like John Scofield and Mike Stern. Creates hip, contemporary dominant sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over C#7#11 chords in jazz contexts. Essential for fusion dominant lines. Emphasize the F## for Lydian dominant characteristic
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Pop, Indie, Folk, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical augmented scale - all whole steps
compatibleScales.rationale: Six-note scale built entirely of whole steps (C#-D#-F-G-A-B), creating ambiguous, floating, dreamlike quality. No perfect 5th creates suspended, unresolved feeling
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist music (Debussy), jazz, film scores, dream sequences. Creates ethereal, mysterious, or surreal atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7+5 or C#aug chords for impressionistic color. Perfect for creating tension that needs resolution. Works best in short phrases
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Folk, Pop, Country, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical diminished scale - alternating whole and half steps
compatibleScales.rationale: Eight-note symmetrical scale with alternating whole-half pattern. Creates tension over dominant 7th chords with rich upper extensions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, modern jazz, fusion. Essential for jazz improvisation over altered dominant chords. Creates sophisticated, complex harmonic color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7b9 chords for bebop and jazz lines. The symmetry allows easy transposition. Perfect for creating outside tension that resolves
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Film Score, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: All 12 notes - ultimate passing tone source
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains all 12 chromatic notes, providing maximum melodic freedom. Every note can function as either chord tone, tension, or passing tone
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, modern jazz, contemporary classical, atonal music. Used for chromatic approach tones, enclosures, and connecting diatonic phrases
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic passing tones to connect chord tones smoothly. Essential for bebop-style eighth note runs. Creates sophisticated melodic lines with strong voice leading
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Contemporary Classical, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - scale tones 1, 2, 3, 5, 6 only
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains only the most consonant notes of C# major (C#, D#, E#, G#, A#), avoiding F# (4th) and B# (7th) which create tension. Completely safe for any C# major harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres; creates bright, optimistic melodies. Especially effective in rock, blues, country, folk, and pop music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for improvisation with guaranteed consonance. Works over I-IV-V progressions in C# major. Ideal for creating singable melodies and memorable hooks
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Blues, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with raised 4th degree (F# becomes F##)
compatibleScales.rationale: Shares all notes except F#→F##, creating a brighter, more ethereal major sound. The #4 creates characteristic dreamy, floating quality
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores, progressive rock, fusion. Common in Joe Satriani and Steve Vai guitar solos. Creates uplifting, transcendent atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#maj7 or C#maj7#11 chords; emphasize the F## for color. Perfect for I chord vamps and modal compositions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord scale - dominant to tonic relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: G# major is the V of C# major; shares 6 of 7 notes (only F#→F##). Creates natural dominant tension that resolves to C#
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; essential for V-I cadences. Fundamental to Western harmony from Bach to contemporary music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when moving to/from G# major chords; emphasizes dominant function. Essential for perfect authentic cadences and establishing tonality
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Folk, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: IV chord scale - subdominant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: F# major is the IV of C# major; shares 6 of 7 notes (only B#→E#). Provides subdominant function and smooth voice leading to tonic
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; essential for I-IV-V progressions. Creates the plagal (IV-I) cadence feeling
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F# major chords and IV-I progressions. Creates smooth subdominant-tonic movement. Essential for folk, rock, and pop songwriting
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Country, Folk, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: vi scale - relative minor, shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: A# natural minor is the relative minor of C# major, containing the exact same seven notes but centered on A#. This vi-I relationship is fundamental
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all styles. The major-minor relationship is essential for understanding modulation and creating emotional contrast
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for switching between major and minor moods without changing key signature. Use over A#m (vi) chords for minor color in major key
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Gospel

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale in C# major system
compatibleScales.rationale: D# Dorian is the second mode of C# major, shares all notes. Provides sophisticated minor color over the ii chord (D#m7)
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, funk, contemporary styles. More versatile than natural minor due to the major 6th. The go-to minor scale for modern improvisation
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for ii-V-I progressions in C# major. Use over D#m7 chords for jazz/funk flavor. Creates smooth modal movement within the key
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Folk, Worship

compatibleScales.relationship: iii modal scale in C# major - mediant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E# Phrygian shares all notes with C# major. The characteristic b2 (F#) creates Spanish/flamenco color over the iii chord
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, flamenco-influenced styles, progressive rock. Creates exotic, dark atmosphere when emphasizing the b2
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E#m chords for Phrygian flavor in C# major context. Perfect for iii-I progressions with Spanish character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Blues, Folk, Country

compatibleScales.relationship: V modal scale - dominant 7th function
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Mixolydian shares all notes with C# major. Perfect for the V7 chord (G#7), providing the dominant 7th sound needed for resolution
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; essential for dominant function. The foundation of blues, rock, and funk dominant chord vamps
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#7 chords for blues-rock or funk flavor. Essential for V7-I resolutions with b7 character. Perfect for dominant vamps
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary, R&B, Neo-Soul

compatibleScales.relationship: vii° modal scale - diminished function
compatibleScales.rationale: B# Locrian shares all notes with C# major but creates diminished chord tension over the vii° (B#dim). Highly unstable, wants to resolve
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Advanced jazz, modern classical. Rarely used as tonal center but valuable for creating tension over half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use briefly over B#m7b5 chords for tension resolving to C#maj. Most effective as passing sonority in ii-V-I progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Country, Folk, Americana

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with b7 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes only B#→B, creating dominant 7th character. Adds bluesy, funky flavor while maintaining C# tonal center
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk, folk. Signature sound of classic rock I7 vamps and jam band grooves. Perfect for blues-rock with major tonality
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 chords and I7 vamps. The B natural is key - emphasize it for Mixolydian character. Essential for blues-rock solos
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Americana

compatibleScales.relationship: Major blues with added #2 and #4 blue notes
compatibleScales.rationale: Major pentatonic with blue notes D## (between D# and E#) and F## (between F# and G#), creating that classic blues-rock major sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues-rock, country, Southern rock. Essential for major blues guitar solos and the Allman Brothers/Skynyrd sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# major and C#7 chords for blues flavor. Bend into the blue notes for authentic blues feeling. Perfect for major blues solos
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Film Score, Contemporary Classical, Minimalist

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - mediant major relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: E major shares 5 notes with C# major. Creates bright, uplifting contrast. Common modulation point and provides major III chord color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Pop, rock, contemporary Christian music. Popular for key changes and major III chord progressions (I-bIII-IV)
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for modulation or over E major chords (bIII) for contemporary pop sound. Creates smooth transition between closely related keys
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: VII major - leading tone major relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: B major shares 5 notes with C# major. Creates bright resolution up by half step. Provides unusual but effective major VII color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contemporary pop, progressive rock. Creates modern, unexpected harmonic movement when used as VII instead of vii°
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for contemporary VII-I progressions or modulation to B major. Creates fresh alternative to traditional dominant resolution

compatibleScales.relationship: ii minor pentatonic - subdominant minor function
compatibleScales.rationale: D# minor pentatonic (D#, F#, G#, A#, C#) fits perfectly over the ii chord and shares 5 notes with C# major. Provides minor color within major key
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk. Common for creating minor flavor over ii chords in major keys. Santana frequently uses this approach
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D#m7 (ii) chords for minor pentatonic flavor in major key context. Perfect for funk and fusion ii-V-I lines

compatibleScales.relationship: Major scale with added #5 passing tone
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds A natural (chromatic passing tone) between A# and B#, creating 8-note scale perfect for bebop eighth-note lines with strong beats on chord tones
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker and Dizzy Gillespie style lines over major chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop-style eighth note runs over C# major chords. The added chromatic note allows chord tones to land on downbeats naturally

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - scale tones 1, b3, 4, 5, b7 only
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains the core minor pentatonic notes (C#, E, F#, G#, B), all consonant with Dorian. Removes the characteristic major 6th (A#) but remains completely compatible
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal minor sound; works in rock, blues, jazz, funk. The most reliable minor scale for improvisation across all genres
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Safe choice for improvisation over C# Dorian; impossible to clash. Use when you want straightforward minor sound without modal complexity
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Funk, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent major scale - C# Dorian is ii mode of B major
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Dorian is the second mode of B major system; shares all notes. Understanding this relationship opens up the entire B major key center
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; provides major context for Dorian. Think of C#m7 as ii in B major rather than i in C# minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in B major when using C# Dorian; opens up ii-V-I possibilities in B major. Essential for understanding modal relationships
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Modal Jazz, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel minor with b6 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (A#→A natural), creating darker, more traditional minor character. Dorian is Aeolian with major 6th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, traditional folk, melancholic rock. More somber than Dorian; the natural minor sound of Aeolian provides darker emotional color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want traditional, classical minor sound. The A natural creates more tension pulling down to G#, while Dorian's A# is brighter
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Fusion, R&B, Soul

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic with added b5 blue note
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds G natural (b5) to minor pentatonic. The blue note creates characteristic blues tension while all other notes align with Dorian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz-blues. Essential for blues-rock guitar solos over minor chords. Creates authentic blues feeling
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7 or C#7 chords for blues flavor. Bend into or through the G natural blue note. Perfect for blues-rock improvisation
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Blues, Alternative, Grunge

compatibleScales.relationship: IV modal scale in B major - subdominant dominant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Mixolydian shares all notes with C# Dorian (both from B major). Creates perfect subdominant-tonic modal movement
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, funk, fusion. Creates sophisticated interplay between C#m7 (i) and F#7 (IV) - common in Herbie Hancock style
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for modal vamps alternating between C# Dorian and F# Mixolydian. Creates the signature i-IV sound of funk and modal jazz
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Funk

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord relationship - dominant to minor tonic
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Mixolydian provides dominant function to C# Dorian tonic. Creates V7-i resolution with modal flavor rather than traditional harmonic minor V-i
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, funk, Latin. The Dorian ii-V sound used extensively by Miles Davis. Softer, less classical than harmonic minor V7-i
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#7 chords resolving to C#m7. Creates modal jazz V-i instead of classical V7-i. Essential for Dorian-based progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with b2 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes D#→D natural, adding Spanish/flamenco character. The b2 creates exotic, darker minor feeling than Dorian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, progressive rock. Creates Spanish or Middle Eastern flavor. Essential for Phrygian-dominant metal riffs
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want Spanish or exotic minor flavor instead of jazz-funk Dorian. The D natural creates characteristic Phrygian sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, Gospel, R&B

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - mediant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: E major shares 5 notes with C# Dorian. Creates major color contrast within Dorian context. Common modulation point
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contemporary pop, rock, indie. The i-bIII movement is extremely popular in modern songwriting. Creates uplifting contrast to minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for C#m to E major progressions (i-bIII). One of the most popular progressions in contemporary music. Provides bright relief from minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary, Smooth Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: vi modal relationship in B major system
compatibleScales.rationale: A# Phrygian shares all notes with C# Dorian (both from B major). Provides minor vi chord color with Phrygian character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, progressive rock. Creates interesting minor-to-minor modal movement (i-vi) with contrasting flavors
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A#m chords for Phrygian flavor in Dorian contexts. Creates sophisticated minor-minor modal relationships
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Indie, Alternative, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: ii natural minor in B major - subdominant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D# natural minor is related to C# Dorian through the B major system. Both are minor scales a whole step apart sharing similar functions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, metal, classical. Creates darker subdominant minor function. Common for minor ii-i progressions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D#m chords for darker minor ii sound. Contrasts with the brighter Dorian tonic for dynamic minor progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Metal, Neoclassical, Film Score, Gothic

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel scale with major 6th and 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Shares major 6th (A#) with Dorian but adds major 7th (B#). Creates sophisticated jazz minor sound with strong leading tone
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. The foundation of jazz minor harmony and source of altered scales. Essential for modern improvisation
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m(maj7) chords for jazz minor color. Opens up melodic minor modal system. More harmonically complex than Dorian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: 4th mode of harmonic minor - Dorian with raised 4th
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Dorian with F##instead of F#, adding harmonic minor flavor while keeping Dorian's major 6th character. Creates exotic color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, world music, progressive styles. Combines Dorian sophistication with harmonic minor exoticism
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m6 or C#m9 chords for exotic Dorian color. The F## creates unique harmonic minor-Dorian hybrid sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Hard Bop, Straight-Ahead Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Dorian with added major 3rd passing tone
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds E# (major 3rd) between E and F#, creating 8-note scale perfect for bebop lines with chord tones on strong beats
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker style minor lines. Creates authentic bebop phrasing
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop eighth-note runs over C#m7 chords. The added chromatic allows natural placement of chord tones on downbeats

compatibleScales.relationship: V Dorian - fifth relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Dorian a perfect 5th above creates modal dominant color. Shares 5 notes with C# Dorian, creating smooth voice leading
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, progressive rock. Creates modal V-i with both chords using Dorian character - less tension than traditional V7-i
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#m7 chords for modal dominant function. Creates the i-v sound with both using Dorian for consistent modal character

compatibleScales.relationship: IV Dorian - subdominant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Dorian a perfect 4th above creates modal subdominant color. Shares multiple notes, creating smooth modal progression
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, funk, fusion. The i-iv modal movement with both chords using Dorian is signature sound of modal funk
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#m7 chords for modal subdominant. Creates smooth i-iv movement with consistent Dorian character throughout

compatibleScales.relationship: Compatible minor pentatonic - avoids the characteristic b2
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains C#, E, F#, G#, B - all consonant with Phrygian. Avoids the D natural (b2) which is Phrygian's most characteristic note
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, metal, blues. Provides safe minor sound without the exotic Phrygian b2. Works when you want standard minor flavor

compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use briefly over D#m7b5 chords (ii°) for tension. Most effective in ii°-V7-i progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Alternative, Indie, Emo

compatibleScales.relationship: bVI major - submediant major relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: A# major shares 5 notes with C# minor. The i-bVI progression is extremely common in rock and pop
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, pop, metal. Creates uplifting contrast to minor. Used in countless rock songs ("Don't Stop Believin'")
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A# major chords (bVI) for bright relief from minor. The i-bVI-bVII-i progression is ubiquitous in rock
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: bVII major - subtonic major relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: B major shares 5 notes with C# minor. The i-bVII progression is one of the most popular in contemporary music
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, pop, indie. Extremely common in modern songwriting. Creates strong, satisfying resolution back to minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over B major chords (bVII) for modern pop/rock sound. The i-bVII-i progression is essential for contemporary writing
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel minor with b2 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes D#→D natural (major 2nd to minor 2nd). Creates Spanish/exotic character with flamenco flavor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, progressive rock. More exotic and dark than natural minor. Essential for Spanish and metal styles
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want Spanish or Middle Eastern minor flavor. The D natural creates characteristic Phrygian tension

compatibleScales.relationship: Diminished scale built on major 3rd - creates tension
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole diminished starting on E# creates sophisticated tension over C# minor. Shares several scale degrees
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. Creates complex harmonic color and tension in minor context
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for outside playing over C#m vamps. Creates sophisticated tension that resolves back to natural minor

compatibleScales.relationship: Sixth mode of harmonic minor - half-diminished with major 2nd
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with b5 (G→G natural), creating half-diminished character while keeping major 2nd
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, modern classical. Creates sophisticated half-diminished color with less extreme dissonance than Locrian
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7b5 chords for jazz half-diminished sound. Less harsh than standard Locrian due to major 2nd

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent major scale - C# Locrian is vii° mode of D major
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Locrian is the seventh mode of D major; shares all notes. Understanding this reveals Locrian's function as vii° in D major
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; Locrian functions as leading tone diminished in major keys. Rarely used as tonic but essential for diminished function
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in D major when using C# Locrian; reveals its function as vii° leading to I. Most useful as passing sonority
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical diminished scale - richer diminished color
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole diminished provides eight-note symmetrical diminished scale. More notes create richer, more complex diminished sound than Locrian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. Essential for sophisticated diminished harmony beyond simple Locrian
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#dim7 or C#m7b5 chords for rich diminished color. Provides more melodic options than seven-note Locrian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Doom Metal, Avant-Garde Metal, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: Locrian with major 2nd - sixth mode of harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes D→D# (b2 to major 2nd). Creates less dissonant half-diminished sound while keeping b5 and b7
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, modern classical. More usable than standard Locrian due to major 2nd. Common in modern jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7b5 chords for jazz half-diminished. Less harsh than standard Locrian; the major 2nd creates smoother lines
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Free Jazz, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic avoiding the most dissonant notes
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic (C#, E, F#, G#, B) avoids Locrian's most problematic notes (D natural and G natural). Provides safer melodic options
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, metal, blues. Useful when Locrian's dissonance is too extreme. Provides familiar minor sound over half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for melodic relief when Locrian's dissonance is overwhelming. Works as simpler alternative over C#m7b5 chords
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Death Metal, Black Metal, Extreme Metal

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale in D major - shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: E Lydian shares all notes with C# Locrian (both from D major). Creates major II chord relationship
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, contemporary classical. The ii-vii° relationship provides interesting modal harmonic movement
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E major chords (II) for Lydian color in Locrian context. Creates sophisticated modal progression
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Gothic, Dark Wave, Post-Punk

compatibleScales.relationship: iii modal scale in D major - shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Dorian shares all notes with C# Locrian (both from D major). Provides minor iii function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal jazz, progressive rock. Creates minor iii-vii° modal movement with contrasting characters
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#m7 chords (iii) for Dorian color. Provides stable minor contrast to Locrian's instability
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: IV modal scale in D major - shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: G major shares all notes with C# Locrian (both from D major). Provides subdominant IV function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; standard subdominant relationship. Creates traditional IV-vii° harmonic movement
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G major chords (IV) for functional subdominant. Shows Locrian's place in functional harmony
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Experimental, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: V modal scale in D major - shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: A Mixolydian shares all notes with C# Locrian (both from D major). Provides dominant V7 function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; standard dominant relationship. The V7-vii° movement shows functional use of Locrian
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A7 chords (V7) for dominant color. Reveals how Locrian fits into functional dominant-diminished relationships
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: vi modal scale in D major - shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: B natural minor shares all notes with C# Locrian (both from D major). Provides minor vi function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; relative minor relationship. Shows Locrian as vii° in the relative minor (B minor)
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bm chords (vi) for natural minor color. Connects Locrian to both major and minor tonal centers
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Contemporary Jazz, Free Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Whole-half diminished for dominant diminished function
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole-half pattern creates different diminished color than half-whole. Useful over C#dim7 when functioning as viio7
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz, film scores. The vii°7 diminished chord uses whole-half diminished for classical voice leading
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#dim7 (vii°7) in classical contexts. Creates traditional diminished 7th chord sound for resolution
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode sharing the b2
compatibleScales.rationale: Phrygian shares the characteristic b2 (D natural) with Locrian. Changes G→G# (b5 to perfect 5th) for more stability
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, progressive rock. Phrygian provides similar dark character but with perfect 5th for more stability
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want Locrian's dark b2 character but need the stability of a perfect 5th. Less unstable than Locrian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: Seventh mode of melodic minor - even more altered than Locrian
compatibleScales.rationale: Super Locrian contains b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7 - maximum alterations. Even more unstable and tense than Locrian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Avant-garde jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. Extreme altered dominant sound. Used by Wayne Shorter and modern jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7alt chords for maximum alteration. More extreme than Locrian; for advanced outside playing
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Progressive Metal, Technical Metal, Math Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent major with added chromatic - bebop approach
compatibleScales.rationale: D bebop major adds chromatic passing tones to D major (parent of C# Locrian). Useful for bebop lines over diminished chords
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Creates chromatic approach to Locrian tones for sophisticated jazz lines
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop-style chromatic approaches to C# Locrian tones. Creates sophisticated voice leading in jazz context

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic a perfect 5th above
compatibleScales.rationale: G# minor pentatonic (G#, B, C#, D#, F#) shares three notes with C# Locrian. Provides alternative melodic material
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, metal, experimental. Creates interesting tension when used over Locrian harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for outside melodic color over C#m7b5 chords. Creates tension through polytonality (G#m over C# Locrian)

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical augmented scale - ambiguous diminished alternative
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone provides different type of instability than Locrian. Both scales create unresolved, floating feeling
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist music, jazz, film scores. Creates dreamy ambiguity as alternative to Locrian's sharp diminished character
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for impressionistic color over C# chords when you want ambiguous rather than diminished character

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel natural minor with b7 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes B#→B (major 7th to minor 7th). Natural minor is less tense and more traditional than harmonic minor's augmented 2nd interval
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, folk, traditional music. Use when harmonic minor's exotic quality is too much. More common in contemporary styles
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want traditional minor without the leading tone. B natural creates softer, less classical feeling than B#
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Neoclassical Metal, Baroque, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel melodic minor with major 6th - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes A→A# (minor 6th to major 6th). Melodic minor smooths out harmonic minor's augmented 2nd while keeping leading tone
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. More sophisticated than harmonic minor. The foundation of modern jazz minor harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use ascending for smoother melodic lines than harmonic minor. The major 6th removes the exotic augmented 2nd interval
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Neoclassical Metal, Shred, Power Metal

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset avoiding the augmented 2nd and leading tone
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains C#, E, F#, G#, B - all consonant with harmonic minor. Avoids both A (b6) and B# (7) to simplify the sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, metal. Provides simple minor sound without harmonic minor's classical complexity
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for straightforward minor improvisation when harmonic minor feels too classical. Saves B# for emphasis
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Spanish Classical, Latin, Tango

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord scale - fifth mode of harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Phrygian Dominant is the fifth mode of C# harmonic minor. Essential for V7-i resolution with exotic flamenco character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, flamenco, metal, Middle Eastern. The traditional dominant scale for harmonic minor. Creates authentic V7-i cadences
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential over G#7 chords for strong V7-i resolution in C# harmonic minor. The signature dominant scale for classical minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Film Score, Soundtrack, Orchestral

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII augmented - third mode of harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E Ionian #5 is the third mode of C# harmonic minor. Creates bright augmented major character over the bIII chord
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contemporary jazz, fusion, film scores. Creates unusual augmented major color in harmonic minor context
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E maj7#5 chords (bIII) for augmented major sound. Adds sophisticated harmonic color to minor progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Gothic, Dark Wave, Industrial

compatibleScales.relationship: ii half-diminished - second mode of harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Locrian ♮6 is the second mode of C# harmonic minor. Provides sophisticated half-diminished ii° sound with major 6th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. More usable than standard Locrian due to major 6th. Common in jazz minor ii-V-i
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#m7b5 chords (ii°) for jazz half-diminished color. The natural 6th (D#) makes it more melodic than Locrian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Classical, Neoclassical, Symphonic Metal

compatibleScales.relationship: bVI Lydian #2 - sixth mode of harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A Lydian #2 is the sixth mode of C# harmonic minor. Creates exotic bright major sound with raised 2nd and 4th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contemporary jazz, fusion, world music. Very unusual lydian variant with augmented 2nd from 1 to #2
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A maj7#11 chords (bVI) for exotic major color. Very advanced; the #2 creates unique harmonic flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: vii° altered dominant - seventh mode of harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: B# Mixolydian b9 b13 is the seventh mode of C# harmonic minor (Super Locrian bb7). Creates complex altered dominant
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Avant-garde jazz, contemporary classical. Extremely altered sound rarely used but theoretically important
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over B#7alt chords for maximum alteration in harmonic minor context. Very advanced diminished dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: vi Dorian #4 - fourth mode of harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A# Dorian #4 is the fourth mode of C# harmonic minor. Combines Dorian character with raised 4th for exotic color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, world music, fusion. Creates sophisticated minor sound with augmented 4th. Combines jazz and exotic flavors
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A#m6 or A#m9 chords (vi) for exotic Dorian color. The raised 4th creates unique harmonic minor character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor blues compatible with harmonic minor tonality
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale (C#, E, F#, G, G#, B) works over harmonic minor. The G natural adds blues flavor to classical minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues-rock with classical minor, neoclassical metal. Combines blues and classical minor for hybrid sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want blues character in classical minor context. Works well in neoclassical metal (Malmsteen style)
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - mediant major relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: E major shares 5 notes with C# harmonic minor. Common as bIII in minor keys, creates uplifting contrast
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, rock, pop. The i-bIII progression is common in minor key compositions. Provides major relief
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E major chords (bIII) for bright contrast to harmonic minor. Common modulation point in classical music
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Gypsy Jazz, Spanish Music, World

compatibleScales.relationship: bVI major - submediant major (Neapolitan relationship)
compatibleScales.rationale: A major shares several notes with C# harmonic minor. The bVI-V7-i progression is common in classical minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, romantic era, film scores. The bVI major creates beautiful contrast before V7-i resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A major chords (bVI) for romantic classical sound. Essential for bVI-V7-i progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: V harmonic major for modal interchange
compatibleScales.rationale: G# harmonic major provides alternative dominant color. Contains the leading tone relationship to C# tonic
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contemporary classical, jazz. Creates unique major dominant with b6, different from Mixolydian or Phrygian Dominant
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G# major chords for alternative dominant color. More exotic than standard dominant scales
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Baroque, Romantic, Chamber Music

compatibleScales.relationship: Similar exotic minor with raised 4th
compatibleScales.rationale: Hungarian minor is harmonic minor with #4 (C#, D#, E, F##, G#, A, B#). Both scales share exotic, Eastern European character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Gypsy music, klezmer, Eastern European folk, film scores. Creates dramatic, exotic minor atmosphere
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for even more exotic minor sound than harmonic minor. The F## adds unique Hungarian/gypsy character

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel Dorian with major 6th and minor 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes A→A# and B#→B (raises 6th, lowers 7th). Creates jazz minor sound without classical leading tone
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, fusion. When you want sophisticated minor without harmonic minor's classical character
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for modern jazz/funk instead of classical harmonic minor sound. The A# brightens and B softens the scale

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel natural minor with b6 and b7 - two notes different
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes A#→A and B#→B (lowers both 6th and 7th). Natural minor is more traditional and less sophisticated than melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical descending, folk, traditional. Melodic minor traditionally descends as natural minor in classical music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use descending for traditional classical melodic minor usage. Natural minor feels more grounded than melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel harmonic minor with b6 - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes A#→A (major 6th to minor 6th). Harmonic minor has exotic augmented 2nd that melodic minor smooths out
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, flamenco, neoclassical metal. Harmonic minor more dramatic; melodic minor more smooth and jazzy
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use harmonic minor when you want exotic V7-i resolution. Melodic minor is smoother for ascending lines
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Contemporary Jazz, Straight-Ahead

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel Dorian with major 7th - one note difference
compatibleScales.rationale: Changes B#→B (major 7th to minor 7th). Dorian is less tense, more funky than melodic minor's leading tone
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, fusion. Dorian is the everyday jazz minor; melodic minor adds leading tone for more sophistication
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Dorian for standard jazz minor. Switch to melodic minor when you want stronger pull to tonic with the B# leading tone
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: ii scale - second mode of melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D# Dorian b2 is the second mode of C# melodic minor. Combines Dorian's major 6th with Phrygian's exotic b2
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary. Sophisticated ii chord scale in melodic minor system. Common in Wayne Shorter compositions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D#m7 chords (ii) for exotic Dorian-Phrygian hybrid. Essential for melodic minor ii-V-i progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII augmented - third mode of melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E Lydian Augmented is the third mode of C# melodic minor. Combines Lydian #4 with augmented 5th for maximum brightness
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. Extremely bright, floating sound. Common in modern jazz over augmented major chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E maj7#5 chords (bIII) for bright augmented sound. Perfect for lydian augmented tonic chords in jazz
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: IV dominant - fourth mode of melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Lydian Dominant is the fourth mode of C# melodic minor. Perfect fusion of Lydian #4 and Mixolydian b7
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, contemporary jazz, progressive rock. Signature scale of Scofield, Stern. Essential for jazz fusion dominant sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over F#7#11 chords (IV7) for fusion dominant. The most important melodic minor mode for modern jazz
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: V dominant - fifth mode of melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Mixolydian b6 is the fifth mode of C# melodic minor. Dominant scale with minor 6th creates dark, sophisticated V7 sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, world music. Creates darker dominant than Mixolydian. Essential for melodic minor V7-i resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#7 chords (V7) for sophisticated dominant resolution. The E natural (b6) creates characteristic tension
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Post-Bop, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: vi half-diminished - sixth mode of melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A# Locrian #2 is the sixth mode of C# melodic minor. Half-diminished scale with major 2nd, more usable than standard Locrian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. The go-to scale for half-diminished chords in jazz. More melodic than standard Locrian
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A#m7b5 chords (vi) for jazz half-diminished sound. The major 2nd (B#) makes it more melodic than Locrian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Metal, Neoclassical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: vii° altered dominant - seventh mode of melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: B# Super Locrian is the seventh mode of C# melodic minor. The most important altered dominant scale in jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, bebop. Essential for altered dominant chords (7#9, 7b9, 7#5, 7b5). Wayne Shorter, John Coltrane signature sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential over B#7alt chords for maximum alteration. Contains all altered extensions (b9, #9, #11, b13). The jazz altered scale
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Blues, Grunge, Alternative

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset avoiding the raised 6th and 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic (C#, E, F#, G#, B) provides simple minor sound. Avoids melodic minor's jazzy A# and B# for rock simplicity
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, metal. Use when melodic minor feels too sophisticated. Provides familiar minor pentatonic sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for straightforward rock/blues over C#m. Melodic minor adds sophistication when needed
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Fusion, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Same scale - jazz melodic minor ascending and descending
compatibleScales.rationale: In jazz, melodic minor is used both ascending and descending (unlike classical). This is the foundation of jazz minor harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary. The melodic minor system is the basis of modern jazz harmony and improvisation
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use ascending and descending over C#m(maj7) chords. Opens up entire melodic minor modal system for improvisation
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Indie, Alternative, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - mediant major relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: E major shares 5 notes with C# melodic minor. Common as bIII in minor keys for bright contrast
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, rock, pop. The i-bIII progression provides major relief in minor contexts
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E major chords (bIII) for bright major color. Common modulation point in melodic minor compositions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary, Smooth Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: VI major - raised submediant (parallel major's vi)
compatibleScales.rationale: A# major shares several notes with C# melodic minor. The VI major chord is common in melodic minor harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Creates bright major VI color unique to melodic minor (not found in harmonic/natural minor)
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A# major chords (VI) for bright major color. The raised 6th of melodic minor enables this major VI chord
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Spanish Music, Gypsy Jazz, World

compatibleScales.relationship: V Phrygian Dominant - alternative dominant from harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: While G# Phrygian Dominant is from harmonic minor, it works as alternative V7 to melodic minor's Mixolydian b6
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Provides Spanish/exotic V7 alternative to melodic minor's more standard Mixolydian b6
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#7 for exotic flamenco dominant instead of jazz Mixolydian b6. Creates different dominant character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Hard Bop, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Melodic minor with added natural 7th for bebop lines
compatibleScales.rationale: Adds B natural between A# and B#, creating 8-note scale. Perfect for bebop lines with chord tones on strong beats
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker style minor lines with melodic minor character
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop eighth-note runs over C#m(maj7). The added chromatic allows natural placement of chord tones

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Locrian ♮6 is the second mode of E harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Locrian ♮6 is built from the second degree of E harmonic minor. Understanding this parent-mode relationship is essential for harmonic minor system improvisation
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, classical, fusion. The Locrian ♮6 mode is favored over standard Locrian due to its major 6th, making it more usable over half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7♭5 (half-diminished) chords when the context is E harmonic minor. The natural 6th (A#) provides better melodic options than Locrian's ♭6
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Same scale - Locrian ♮6 is the preferred scale for half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.rationale: The natural 6th makes this mode far more melodically useful than standard Locrian. Essential for jazz half-diminished chord voicings and improvis
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. The go-to scale for minor ii-V-i progressions where half-diminished chords appear. Used extensively in modal jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary scale choice for C#m7♭5 chords in jazz contexts. The A# (natural 6th) creates smooth voice leading and avoids the harsh ♭6 of standard Locrian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Closely related - shares 6 of 7 notes with C# Locrian ♮6
compatibleScales.rationale: E melodic minor differs only in the 7th degree (D# vs D natural). Both scales work well over similar harmonic contexts and share the important natural 6th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. When the harmonic context shifts between melodic and harmonic minor systems, both scales remain viable over half-diminished function
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Substitute E melodic minor when you want the brighter D# leading tone instead of D natural. Useful for creating melodic variety over extended C#m7♭5 passages
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 6th degree (A natural vs A#)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Locrian with ♭6 (A natural) vs Locrian ♮6 with A#. The natural 6th version is strongly preferred in jazz for its improved melodic character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, metal. Standard Locrian is less common in jazz due to its harsh ♭6. Used more in classical diminished contexts and dark metal riffs
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use standard Locrian when you specifically want the darker ♭6 sound, or in classical harmonic minor contexts. For jazz, strongly prefer Locrian ♮6
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Doom Metal, Avant-Garde Metal, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: Built from the major 6th - emphasizes the characteristic natural 6th of Locrian ♮6
compatibleScales.rationale: A# minor pentatonic (A#-C#-D#-E#-G#) relates strongly to the raised 6th degree. Provides a simple, consonant framework over the complex Locrian ♮6 harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz-rock fusion, contemporary styles. Simplifies the Locrian ♮6 sound into an accessible pentatonic framework while maintaining the raised 6th character

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord scale - dominant function in E harmonic minor

compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#7 chords when establishing E harmonic minor tonality. Creates the characteristic V7-i resolution with exotic b9 and b13 alterations

compatibleScales.relationship: bVII augmented - third mode of E harmonic minor

compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over B maj7#5 chords (bVII) for unusual augmented major color. Works in contexts where E harmonic minor harmony permits augmented tonic substitutions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: bII - second degree relationship in harmonic minor system
compatibleScales.rationale: D Dorian #4 (fourth mode of E harmonic minor) shares significant harmonic material. Creates interesting modal interchange possibilities

compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: IV - fifth mode of E harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Lydian #2 (fifth mode) features both #2 (G##) and #4 (B#), creating extremely bright major character. Very rare and exotic sound

compatibleScales.relationship: bVI dominant - sixth mode of E harmonic minor

compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Gypsy Jazz, Spanish Music, World Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale sharing diminished character
compatibleScales.rationale: The half-whole diminished scale shares the diminished triad (C#-E-G) with Locrian ♮6. Provides symmetrical approach to half-diminished harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. The symmetrical nature allows for pattern-based improvisation over half-diminished chords with diminished color

compatibleScales.relationship: Simplified minor framework avoiding altered tones
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic (C#-E-F#-G#-B) provides safe minor sound. Avoids the complex altered notes of Locrian ♮6 while maintaining minor character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, fusion. When Locrian ♮6 sounds too jazzy or complex, minor pentatonic offers familiar minor vocabulary
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bluesy approach to C#m7♭5 chords. Works when you want straightforward minor sound without harmonic minor complexity
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Ionian #5 is the third mode of A# harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Ionian #5 is built from the third degree of A# harmonic minor. This parent-mode relationship is the foundation for understanding how augmented major functions in harmonic minor contexts
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical, film scores. The augmented major sound provides exotic major coloration in minor key contexts, rare in traditional Western harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# maj7#5 or C# aug chords when the harmonic center is A# harmonic minor. The augmented 5th (G##/double sharp) creates characteristic bright tension
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Shares augmented triad - both built on C# E# G## augmented core
compatibleScales.rationale: The symmetrical augmented scale and Ionian #5 both contain the C# augmented triad. Augmented scale provides more chromatic possibilities while maintaining the augmented character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, avant-garde, contemporary classical. Augmented scale's symmetry complements Ionian #5's diatonic structure, offering both inside and outside improvisation options
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use augmented scale over C# aug or C# maj7#5 for more chromatic, outside sound. Alternate with Ionian #5 for contrast between diatonic and symmetrical approaches
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 5th degree (G# vs G##)
compatibleScales.rationale: Lydian's natural 5th (G#) vs Ionian #5's augmented 5th (G##/double sharp) is the only difference. Both share the characteristic bright #4 and major tonality
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, progressive. When the augmented 5th becomes too tense, Lydian offers a more stable bright major alternative while maintaining the #4 color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Lydian for more stable major sound when G## creates too much tension. Both scales work over maj7 chords, with Lydian being more consonant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Built from the major 3rd - shares most tones with Ionian #5
compatibleScales.rationale: E# melodic minor contains most notes of C# Ionian #5. The III melodic minor relationship is common in augmented major harmony, providing smooth voice leading
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. The III melodic minor over augmented I provides sophisticated harmonic color, used in modern jazz reharmonization
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# maj7#5 chords for melodic minor color. The III melodic minor creates smooth connection between augmented and minor modal centers

compatibleScales.relationship: bVI dominant - fifth mode of A# harmonic minor system

compatibleScales.relationship: Shares augmented quality - both create suspended, floating augmented harmony
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone scale's complete symmetry and augmented intervals complement Ionian #5's augmented 5th. Both create ambiguous, unresolved augmented tension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist classical (Debussy), jazz, avant-garde. Whole tone provides maximum ambiguity over augmented chords when you want complete tonal suspension
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# aug for completely ambiguous, floating sound. The lack of half-steps creates impressionist color, perfect for film scores and jazz reharmonization
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Ambient

compatibleScales.relationship: IV half-diminished - second mode of A# harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F## Locrian ♮6 shares the A# harmonic minor parent scale. Creates interesting iv half-diminished to I augmented progression in contemporary harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. The half-diminished to augmented progression provides sophisticated modal movement within harmonic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F##m7♭5 (iv) leading to C# maj7#5 (I). Explores the full harmonic minor system through different modal colors

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel scale - natural 5th instead of augmented 5th

compatibleScales.relationship: VII - fourth mode of A# harmonic minor

compatibleScales.relationship: IV dominant - sixth mode of A# harmonic minor

compatibleScales.relationship: Simplified major framework avoiding the augmented 5th

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, pop, blues. When Ionian #5 sounds too exotic, major pentatonic offers familiar major vocabulary without augmented tension

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Dorian #4 is the fourth mode of G# harmonic minor

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 4th degree (F# vs F##)

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, fusion. Standard Dorian is more common, but Dorian #4 offers unique harmonic minor color. Use Dorian #4 for more exotic minor improvisation

compatibleScales.relationship: III dominant - fifth mode of G# harmonic minor

compatibleScales.relationship: Simplified minor framework avoiding the raised 4th

compatibleScales.relationship: IV - fifth mode of G# harmonic minor

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset emphasizing dominant 7th character

compatibleScales.rationale: D Phrygian Dominant is the dominant scale in F harmonic minor. Creates exotic Spanish dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, jazz. The authentic harmonic minor V7 provides strong resolution to F minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D7 (bII) for Spanish dominant character within F harmonic minor context
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Spanish Music, Gypsy Jazz, Latin Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII dominant - sixth mode of F harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Eb Mixolydian b9 b13 provides dark altered dominant. The b9 and b13 create exotic tension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, gypsy jazz. Dark dominant typical of harmonic minor system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Eb7 (bIII7) for gypsy/Spanish dominant within F harmonic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Gypsy Jazz, Spanish Music, World Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale sharing bright, ambiguous character
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone's complete symmetry complements Lydian #2's extreme brightness. Both create suspended, unresolved major harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist classical, avant-garde jazz. Maximum ambiguity and brightness
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole tone for completely ambiguous bright sound. Lydian #2 maintains diatonic function while whole tone suspends tonality
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Ambient

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical augmented scale sharing bright character
compatibleScales.rationale: Augmented scale's symmetry and bright quality relate to Lydian #2's extreme major brightness
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Provides symmetrical alternative to Lydian #2's diatonic structure
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use augmented scale for chromatic brightness over C# maj7#5. Lydian #2 for diatonic brightness
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Mixolydian b9 b13 is the sixth mode (bVI7) of E harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Mixolydian b9 b13 derives from the sixth degree of E harmonic minor. The b9 (D) and b13 (A) create the characteristic dark, Spanish/gypsy dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, gypsy jazz, contemporary jazz, Middle Eastern music. The altered dominant typical of harmonic minor, creating exotic tension over dominant 7th chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 chords when the harmonic center is E harmonic minor. The b9 and b13 provide Spanish/Arabic dominant character distinct from standard Mixolydian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Flamenco, Gypsy Jazz, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs in the 2nd and 6th degrees
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Mixolydian has major 2nd (D#) and major 6th (A#), while Mixolydian b9 b13 has b2 (D) and b6 (A). Standard Mix is more conventional blues/rock dominant
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk vs flamenco, gypsy. Mixolydian provides familiar dominant sound; Mixolydian b9 b13 gives exotic Spanish character
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use standard Mixolydian for blues/rock. Switch to Mixolydian b9 b13 for flamenco/Spanish/gypsy dominant character over C#7
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: Closely related harmonic minor dominant scales
compatibleScales.rationale: Both are harmonic minor dominant modes with b2. Phrygian Dominant (5th mode) is the V7, Mixolydian b9 b13 (6th mode) is the bVI7. Very similar Spanish sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, Spanish music, metal. Both provide authentic Spanish dominant character from harmonic minor system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Both work over C#7 for Spanish sound. Phrygian Dominant slightly brighter (major 3rd emphasis), Mixolydian b9 b13 darker (b13 emphasis)
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Spanish Music, Gypsy Jazz, Latin Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Alternative altered dominant from melodic minor system
compatibleScales.rationale: Altered scale (7th mode of melodic minor) has all alterations: b9, #9, #11, b13. More chromatic than Mixolydian b9 b13 but similar dark dominant function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop, fusion. Altered scale is the jazz standard for altered dominants, while Mixolydian b9 b13 is the flamenco/Spanish version
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use altered for bebop/jazz altered dominant. Use Mixolydian b9 b13 for Spanish/flamenco altered dominant. Different flavors of dark V7
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Post-Bop, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues framework applicable to dominant chords
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale can work over dominant 7th chords. The b3 and b5 create bluesy tension similar to b9 and b13 alterations
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz-blues. Blues scale provides gritty dominant sound, less exotic than Mixolydian b9 b13
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use blues scale for gritty blues-rock dominant. Use Mixolydian b9 b13 for Spanish/flamenco exotic dominant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Funk

compatibleScales.relationship: Target tonic - the i chord this dominant relates to
compatibleScales.rationale: Mixolydian b9 b13 on C# functions as bVI7 in E harmonic minor. Understanding the relationship to E minor tonic is essential for harmonic context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, classical. The bVI7-i progression is common in Spanish music and provides exotic resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 (bVI7) when resolving to Em (i). Creates characteristic Spanish/gypsy progression
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Folk, Gothic, Dark Wave

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord scale sharing b2 character
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Phrygian (V in E natural minor) shares the dark b2 character. Both scales create Spanish/Middle Eastern color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, Middle Eastern, metal. Phrygian provides minor equivalent of Mixolydian b9 b13's dominant color
