compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use G# Phrygian over G#m (V) for Spanish minor sound. Relates to Mixolydian b9 b13 through shared E harmonic minor system
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Thrash Metal, Death Metal, Progressive Metal

compatibleScales.relationship: IV - fourth mode of E harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: B Dorian #4 shares E harmonic minor parent. The raised 4th creates sophisticated minor color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Part of the E harmonic minor modal system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Explore over Bm7 (IV) within E harmonic minor progressions using Mixolydian b9 b13
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: bVII augmented - third mode of E harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D Ionian #5 shares the parent scale. Provides augmented major color in harmonic minor context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contemporary classical, experimental. Augmented major rare in traditional harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D maj7#5 (bVII) for exotic augmented color within E harmonic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: II half-diminished - second mode of E harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Locrian ♮6 is the ii in E harmonic minor. Creates potential ii-bVI7 progressions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Half-diminished to altered dominant provides sophisticated harmonic movement
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#m7♭5 (ii) before C#7 (bVI7) in E harmonic minor contexts
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: V - fifth mode of E harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A# Lydian #2 shares E harmonic minor. Both #2 and #4 create extreme brightness
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Avant-garde, contemporary classical. Extremely bright, rare in practical application
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Explore over A# maj7 (V) for maximum brightness within harmonic minor system
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical diminished scale over dominant 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole-half diminished (dominant diminished) works over dominant 7th chords. Provides symmetrical alternative to Mixolydian b9 b13
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Symmetrical approach to dominant 7th alteration
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole-half diminished over C#7 for symmetrical dominant sound. Mixolydian b9 b13 for Spanish/diatonic approach
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Bebop, Free Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Dorian b2 is the second mode of B melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Dorian b2 is built from the second degree of B melodic minor. This parent-mode relationship is fundamental to understanding melodic minor modal harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. Dorian b2 combines minor quality with both dark (b2) and bright (major 6th) characteristics, creating sophisticated minor color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7 or C#m9 chords when the harmonic center is B melodic minor. The b2 (D) provides Phrygian darkness while the major 6th (A#) maintains Dorian sophistication
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 2nd degree (D# vs D natural)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Dorian has major 2nd (D#) while Dorian b2 has minor 2nd (D). The b2 adds Phrygian darkness to Dorian's otherwise bright minor character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, fusion. Standard Dorian is more common and versatile. Dorian b2 provides exotic alternative when you want darker, more Spanish-influenced minor sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use standard Dorian for typical jazz/funk minor. Switch to Dorian b2 for darker, more exotic flavor with Spanish/Phrygian character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Closely related - differs only in the 6th degree (A natural vs A#)
compatibleScales.rationale: Phrygian has b6 (A natural) while Dorian b2 has major 6th (A#). Both share the dark b2, but Dorian b2 is brighter due to the major 6th
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Spanish music, metal, jazz. Phrygian is darker and more traditional Spanish. Dorian b2 is jazzier with its major 6th
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Phrygian for traditional Spanish/flamenco darkness. Use Dorian b2 for jazz/fusion minor with Spanish flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Thrash Metal, Death Metal, Progressive Metal

compatibleScales.relationship: Simplified minor framework avoiding the b2
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic provides safe minor sound without the exotic b2. Uses major 2nd (D#) and avoids Dorian b2's Phrygian character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, folk. When Dorian b2 sounds too exotic or jazzy, minor pentatonic offers familiar, accessible minor vocabulary
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use minor pentatonic for straightforward rock/blues minor. Dorian b2 adds sophistication when the musical context supports it
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: II augmented - third mode of B melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D# Lydian Augmented is the third mode of B melodic minor. Combines Lydian #4 with augmented 5th for maximum brightness, connecting through shared parent scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. Extremely bright augmented major sound typical of melodic minor system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D# maj7#5 (II) for bright augmented color. Understanding this modal relationship reveals melodic minor system connections
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: III dominant - fourth mode of B melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E# Lydian Dominant (fourth mode) is the signature melodic minor dominant. Combines Lydian #4 with Mixolydian b7 for jazz fusion sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, contemporary jazz. The most important melodic minor mode for modern jazz dominant function. Signature scale of Scofield, Stern, Metheny
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E#7#11 (III7) for fusion dominant. The #4 and b7 combination defines modern jazz dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: IV dominant - fifth mode of B melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Mixolydian b6 (fifth mode) creates dark sophisticated dominant. The minor 6th provides unique tension unavailable in standard Mixolydian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, world music. Creates darker dominant than Mixolydian, essential for melodic minor V7-i resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#7 (IV7) for sophisticated dark dominant. The D natural (b6) creates characteristic melodic minor tension
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: V half-diminished - sixth mode of B melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Locrian #2 (sixth mode) is the go-to half-diminished scale. Major 2nd makes it far more usable than standard Locrian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Essential for half-diminished chords in jazz. More melodic than standard Locrian or Locrian ♮6
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#m7♭5 (V) for jazz half-diminished sound. The major 2nd (A#) makes it more melodic than Locrian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: VI altered dominant - seventh mode of B melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A# Super Locrian is THE jazz altered dominant scale. Contains all altered extensions (b9, #9, #11, b13) essential for bebop and modern jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop, fusion. The signature altered dominant of Wayne Shorter, John Coltrane, McCoy Tyner. Essential for modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential over A#7alt (VI7alt) for maximum alteration. The most important scale for jazz altered dominant chords
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Post-Bop, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Same parent scale - understanding the melodic minor system
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding B melodic minor as the source of Dorian b2 is essential. In jazz, melodic minor is used both ascending and descending
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary. The melodic minor system is the foundation of modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in terms of B melodic minor when using Dorian b2. Opens up the entire melodic minor modal system
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Dorian with added major 3rd for bebop lines
compatibleScales.rationale: Bebop Dorian adds E# between E and F#, creating 8-note scale. Combines with Dorian b2 for chromatic bebop minor vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker/Bud Powell style minor lines
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop eighth-note runs over C#m7. Alternate with Dorian b2 for exotic flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Hard Bop, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - mediant relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: E major shares several notes with C# Dorian b2. The bIII major provides bright contrast in minor contexts
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, rock, pop. The i-bIII progression is common for major relief in minor keys
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E major (bIII) for bright major color. Common modulation point from minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Indie, Alternative, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Lydian Augmented is the third mode of A melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Lydian Augmented derives from the third degree of A melodic minor. Combines Lydian's #4 with augmented 5th, creating the brightest possible major scale sonority
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical, film scores. One of the brightest scales in existence, used for maximum major-key luminosity and floating augmented harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# maj7#5 or C# maj7#11#5 chords when the harmonic center is A melodic minor. Both F## (#4) and G## (aug 5th) create characteristic bright, suspended quality
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 5th degree (G# vs G##)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Lydian has natural 5th while Lydian Augmented has augmented 5th. Both share the characteristic bright #4, but augmented 5th adds suspension and ambiguity
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores, fusion. Lydian is more stable and usable. Lydian Augmented provides maximum brightness when suspension is desired
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Lydian for stable bright major sound. Use Lydian Augmented when you want floating, suspended augmented quality with #4 brightness
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale sharing augmented triad core
compatibleScales.rationale: Both contain C# augmented triad (C#-E#-G##). Augmented scale provides symmetrical chromatic approach while Lydian Augmented offers diatonic structure
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical, avant-garde. Augmented scale's symmetry complements Lydian Augmented's diatonic brightness
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use augmented scale for chromatic symmetrical approach over C# aug. Use Lydian Augmented for diatonic melodic approach. Both work over maj7#5 chords
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale sharing augmented quality and brightness
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone's complete symmetry and augmented intervals relate to Lydian Augmented's augmented 5th. Both create suspended, floating major harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist classical (Debussy), jazz, film scores. Whole tone provides maximum ambiguity, Lydian Augmented maintains diatonic function
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole tone for completely ambiguous floating sound. Use Lydian Augmented when you want bright suspended sound with diatonic melodic structure
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Ambient

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale understanding - melodic minor system
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding A melodic minor as the source reveals the entire modal system. Lydian Augmented as III creates the brightest color in melodic minor harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary. Melodic minor system is foundation of modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in A melodic minor when using Lydian Augmented. Opens up entire melodic minor modal possibilities
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: II - second mode of A melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: B Dorian b2 shares A melodic minor parent. Creates ii-III progression using both modes of the melodic minor system
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. The ii-III progression explores different colors within the same melodic minor parent
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bm7 (ii) before C# maj7#5 (III). Explores melodic minor system through connected modal movement
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bII dominant - fourth mode of A melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D Lydian Dominant (fourth mode) is the signature melodic minor dominant. Creates tritone relationship with Lydian Augmented
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, contemporary jazz. Essential for modern jazz dominant function and melodic minor harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D7#11 (bII7) for fusion dominant. The tritone substitution relationship creates sophisticated harmonic movement
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII dominant - fifth mode of A melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E Mixolydian b6 provides dark dominant color. Shares A melodic minor parent scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, world music. Dark sophisticated dominant characteristic of melodic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E7 (bIII7) for dark dominant within A melodic minor system
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: IV half-diminished - sixth mode of A melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Locrian #2 shares the parent scale. Creates half-diminished color within melodic minor system
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Essential half-diminished scale for jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#m7♭5 (IV) within A melodic minor progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: V altered - seventh mode of A melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Super Locrian is THE altered dominant. Contains all alterations for bebop/jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop, fusion. Essential for altered dominant chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#7alt (V7alt) for maximum alteration in A melodic minor context
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Post-Bop, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel scale - resolution from augmented tension
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard C# major provides resolution from Lydian Augmented's augmented and #4 tension. Natural 4th and 5th create stable major sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres. Return to standard major after Lydian Augmented creates satisfying resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# major to resolve Lydian Augmented's floating quality. Chromatic movement G##→G# and F##→F# provides smooth voice leading
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Folk, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: Simplified major avoiding altered tones
compatibleScales.rationale: Major pentatonic uses natural 4th and 5th, providing safe major sound without Lydian Augmented's complexity
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, pop, blues. When Lydian Augmented is too exotic, major pentatonic offers familiar vocabulary
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for accessible major sound. Avoids the #4 and aug 5th complexity
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Blues, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Lydian Dominant is the fourth mode (IV7) of G# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Lydian Dominant is THE signature scale of melodic minor harmony. Combines Lydian's #4 with Mixolydian's b7, creating the definitive jazz fusion dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, contemporary jazz, progressive rock. The most important melodic minor mode for modern jazz. Signature sound of John Scofield, Mike Stern, Pat Metheny, Steve Vai
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7#11 chords in any context. Essential for modern jazz fusion dominant. The #4 (F##) with b7 (B) defines contemporary jazz dominant vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 4th degree (F# vs F##)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Mixolydian has natural 4th while Lydian Dominant has #4. Both have dominant 7th character (b7), but Lydian Dominant is brighter and more modern
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock/blues vs jazz fusion. Mixolydian for traditional blues/rock dominant, Lydian Dominant for sophisticated jazz fusion sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Mixolydian for blues/rock dominant. Use Lydian Dominant for jazz fusion or when you want brighter, more modern dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 7th degree (B# vs B)
compatibleScales.rationale: Lydian has major 7th (B#) while Lydian Dominant has minor 7th (B). Both share the bright #4, but Lydian Dominant adds dominant 7th tension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Lydian for major 7th chords, Lydian Dominant for dominant 7th chords. Both provide #4 brightness
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Lydian over maj7 chords, Lydian Dominant over dominant 7th chords. The #4 brightness works in both contexts
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: Dominant with added major 7th for bebop lines
compatibleScales.rationale: Bebop dominant adds B# between B and C#, creating 8-note scale. Perfect for bebop lines over dominant 7th chords with chord tones on downbeats
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker/Dizzy Gillespie style dominant lines
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for bebop eighth-note runs over C#7. Combines with Lydian Dominant for modern jazz vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Contemporary Jazz, Hard Bop

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale understanding - melodic minor system foundation
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding G# melodic minor as source is essential. Lydian Dominant as IV7 is the most important scale in modern jazz dominant harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. The melodic minor system revolutionized modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in G# melodic minor when using Lydian Dominant. Opens entire melodic minor modal system
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: II - second mode of G# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A# Dorian b2 shares G# melodic minor parent. Creates ii-IV7 progression within melodic minor system
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Minor ii to Lydian Dominant IV7 is common in melodic minor progressions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A#m7 (ii) before C#7#11 (IV7). Explores connected modal colors
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: III augmented - third mode of G# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: B# Lydian Augmented (third mode) provides maximum brightness. Shares melodic minor parent
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Extremely bright augmented major
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over B# maj7#5 (III) for bright augmented. Modal connection within melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: V dominant - fifth mode of G# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D# Mixolydian b6 provides dark dominant. The V7 to IV7 progression uses both dominant scales
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Dark sophisticated dominant typical of melodic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D#7 (V7) within G# melodic minor. Creates modal variety with Lydian Dominant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: VI half-diminished - sixth mode of G# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E# Locrian #2 shares melodic minor parent. Essential half-diminished scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Go-to scale for half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over E#m7♭5 (VI) in G# melodic minor context
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: VII altered - seventh mode of G# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F## Super Locrian is the altered dominant. Maximum alteration for jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop. Essential for altered dominant chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F##7alt (VII7alt) for complete alteration
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Post-Bop, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale over dominant with #4
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone contains #4 and provides completely ambiguous dominant sound. Alternative to Lydian Dominant's diatonic approach
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist, jazz, avant-garde. Maximum ambiguity over dominant chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole tone for completely suspended dominant sound. Lydian Dominant for diatonic fusion approach
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Ambient

compatibleScales.relationship: IV major - emphasizes the characteristic #4
compatibleScales.rationale: F## major highlights the defining #4 of Lydian Dominant. Provides major color at the #4 degree
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Creates interesting harmonic color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F## major (IV) to emphasize the #4 character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Instrumental Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Mixolydian b6 is the fifth mode (V7) of F# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Mixolydian b6 functions as the V7 dominant in F# melodic minor. The b6 (A natural) creates darker, more sophisticated dominant than standard Mixolydian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, world music, Indian classical. The 'Hindu scale' name reflects its use in Indian music. Essential for melodic minor V7-i resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 chords when resolving to F# melodic minor (i). The A natural (b6) creates characteristic dark melodic minor dominant tension
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 6th degree (A# vs A natural)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Mixolydian has major 6th (A#) while Mixolydian b6 has minor 6th (A). The b6 creates darker, more exotic dominant character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues/rock vs jazz/world. Mixolydian for traditional blues/rock, Mixolydian b6 for sophisticated jazz or world music dominant
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Mixolydian for bright blues/rock dominant. Use Mixolydian b6 for darker, jazzier, or world music dominant flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: Related mode - differs in 3rd and 7th degrees
compatibleScales.rationale: Dorian has minor 3rd and major 7th; Mixolydian b6 has major 3rd and minor 7th. Both share the characteristic b6 (A natural) but different qualities
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Both scales share dark b6 character but serve different functions (minor vs dominant)
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Recognize the shared b6 character. Dorian for minor, Mixolydian b6 for dominant with similar dark color
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale and target tonic
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding F# melodic minor reveals the V7-i resolution function. Mixolydian b6 as V7 creates authentic melodic minor dominant resolution
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. The melodic minor V7-i is fundamental to modern jazz minor harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 (V7) resolving to F#m (i). Essential for melodic minor dominant function
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: II - second mode of F# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Dorian b2 shares F# melodic minor parent. Creates ii-V7 progression within melodic minor system
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. The ii-V7 using melodic minor modes
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#m7 (ii) before C#7 (V7) in F# melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII augmented - third mode of F# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A Lydian Augmented shares the parent scale. Provides bright augmented color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Maximum brightness within melodic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A maj7#5 (bIII) for bright contrast to dark Mixolydian b6
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: bVII dominant - fourth mode of F# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: B Lydian Dominant is the signature melodic minor dominant. Both scales are dominant modes of melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion. Two essential melodic minor dominant scales
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over B7#11 (bVII7) for fusion dominant. Compare with Mixolydian b6's darker character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: VI half-diminished - sixth mode of F# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Locrian #2 shares the parent. Essential half-diminished scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Go-to half-diminished scale
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7♭5 (VI) in F# melodic minor context
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: VII altered - seventh mode of F# melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D# Super Locrian provides altered dominant. Maximum alteration for jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop. Essential altered dominant scale
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D#7alt (VII7alt) for complete alteration
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Post-Bop, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Alternative dominant with b2 and b6
compatibleScales.rationale: Phrygian Dominant (from harmonic minor) shares similar exotic dominant character. Both have dark alterations
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco vs jazz. Phrygian Dominant for Spanish, Mixolydian b6 for jazz/world
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Phrygian Dominant for flamenco, Mixolydian b6 for jazz/melodic minor dominant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Spanish Music, Gypsy Jazz, Latin Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues framework over dominant chords
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale can work over dominant chords. Provides bluesy alternative to melodic minor sophistication
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock. Simpler approach than Mixolydian b6
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use blues scale for gritty blues-rock dominant. Mixolydian b6 for sophisticated jazz dominant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Funk

compatibleScales.relationship: Built from the b6 - emphasizes the characteristic minor 6th
compatibleScales.rationale: A minor pentatonic highlights the defining b6 of Mixolydian b6. Provides pentatonic simplicity
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues with exotic flavor. Simplifies the complex harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want the b6 character with pentatonic simplicity
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Alternative, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Aeolian b5 is the sixth mode (vi) of E melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Aeolian b5 (also called Locrian #2) is THE go-to scale for half-diminished chords in jazz. The natural 2nd (D#) makes it far more melodically usable than standard Locrian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical, fusion. The definitive half-diminished scale, essential for minor ii-V-i progressions. More melodic than both standard Locrian and Locrian ♮6
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7♭5 chords in any jazz context. The major 2nd (D#) provides smooth melodic motion, making this the preferred half-diminished scale in modern jazz
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode - differs only in the 2nd degree (D vs D#)
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard Locrian has b2 (D) while Aeolian b5 has major 2nd (D#). The natural 2nd makes Aeolian b5 vastly superior for melodic improvisation
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical vs jazz. Locrian for theoretical diminished contexts, Aeolian b5 for practical jazz half-diminished use
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Aeolian b5 for jazz half-diminished chords. Standard Locrian is rarely used due to its harsh b2
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Doom Metal, Avant-Garde Metal, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: Alternative half-diminished scale from harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Locrian ♮6 (from harmonic minor) has b2 and natural 6th. Aeolian b5 has natural 2nd and b6. Both improve on standard Locrian but in different ways
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Harmonic minor vs melodic minor systems. Locrian ♮6 for harmonic minor, Aeolian b5 for melodic minor contexts
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Aeolian b5 in jazz/melodic minor contexts. Use Locrian ♮6 in classical/harmonic minor contexts
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical diminished scale sharing half-diminished function
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole diminished scale provides symmetrical approach to half-diminished harmony. More chromatic than Aeolian b5 but shares diminished character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Symmetrical alternative for half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use half-whole diminished for symmetrical, pattern-based approach. Aeolian b5 for diatonic melodic lines
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Bebop, Free Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale understanding - melodic minor system
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding E melodic minor as source reveals the entire modal system. Aeolian b5 as vi is the most important scale for half-diminished function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Essential for understanding modern jazz harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think in E melodic minor when using Aeolian b5. Opens melodic minor modal possibilities
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: II - second mode of E melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Dorian b2 shares E melodic minor parent. Creates ii-vi progression within melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Modal connections within melodic minor system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#m7 (ii) before C#m7♭5 (vi) in E melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII augmented - third mode of E melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Lydian Augmented provides maximum brightness. Shares melodic minor parent
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Bright augmented major contrast
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G# maj7#5 (bIII) for bright color
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: IV dominant - fourth mode of E melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A# Lydian Dominant is the signature melodic minor dominant. Essential for jazz fusion
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion, contemporary. The most important melodic minor dominant
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A#7#11 (IV7) for fusion dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: V dominant - fifth mode of E melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: B Mixolydian b6 creates dark sophisticated dominant. Shares parent scale
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, world. Dark dominant characteristic of melodic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over B7 (V7) for dark dominant within E melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: VII altered - seventh mode of E melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: D# Super Locrian provides altered dominant. Maximum alteration
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop. Essential altered dominant scale
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D#7alt (VII7alt) for complete alteration
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Post-Bop, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Simplified minor framework avoiding b5
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic provides safe minor sound without the b5 diminished character. Uses perfect 5th (G#) instead
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues. When Aeolian b5 sounds too jazzy, minor pentatonic offers familiar sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use for simple minor approach. Aeolian b5 for jazz half-diminished sophistication
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Related through E minor tonality
compatibleScales.rationale: E natural minor relates to E melodic minor. Both provide minor context
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, rock. E natural minor for traditional minor harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Compare E natural minor with E melodic minor parent of Aeolian b5
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Folk, Gothic, Dark Wave

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - C# Super Locrian is the seventh mode of D melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Super Locrian (Altered Scale) is THE definitive altered dominant scale in jazz. Contains all possible alterations: b9 (D), #9 (D##/E), #11 (G), b13 (A)
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop, fusion. The signature scale of Wayne Shorter, John Coltrane, McCoy Tyner. Essential for modern jazz altered dominant function - the most important scale in bebop and post-bop
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential over C#7alt, C#7#9, C#7b9, C#7#11b13 - any altered dominant chord. The ultimate jazz altered scale containing all alterations simultaneously
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical dominant diminished scale
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole-half diminished (dominant diminished) is the symmetrical alternative for altered dominants. Contains b9, #9, #11, 13 - slightly different alterations than Super Locrian
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Symmetrical approach vs Super Locrian's diatonic melodic minor approach
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole-half diminished for symmetrical patterns over C#7. Use Super Locrian for melodic minor-based lines. Both work over altered dominants
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Bebop, Free Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Alternative altered dominant from harmonic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Phrygian Dominant (5th mode of harmonic minor) also has b9 and functions as altered dominant, but with major 3rd focus and Spanish character vs Super Locrian's jazz character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco vs bebop/jazz. Phrygian Dominant for Spanish altered dominant, Super Locrian for jazz altered dominant
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Phrygian Dominant for Spanish/flamenco V7. Use Super Locrian for bebop/jazz altered V7. Different flavors of altered dominant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Spanish Music, Gypsy Jazz, Latin Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Alternative altered dominant from harmonic minor with b9 and b13
compatibleScales.rationale: Mixolydian b9 b13 (6th mode of harmonic minor) shares b9 and b13 alterations. Less chromatic than Super Locrian but similar dark dominant function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, gypsy jazz vs bebop. Both provide dark altered dominant but different systems (harmonic minor vs melodic minor)
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Mixolydian b9 b13 for Spanish/gypsy altered dominant. Use Super Locrian for bebop altered dominant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Gypsy Jazz, Spanish Music, World Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale understanding - altered scale derivation
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding D melodic minor (built on the b9!) as source is crucial. Super Locrian as VII is the most important altered dominant scale in all of jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. The melodic minor a half-step above the root is the key to altered dominant harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think 'melodic minor up a half-step' for altered dominants. This relationship is fundamental to modern jazz
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bII - second mode of D melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: E Dorian b2 shares D melodic minor parent. Creates bII-V7alt tritone substitution possibilities
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. Understanding modal relationships within melodic minor enables sophisticated reharmonization
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Explore over Em7 (bII) as tritone sub approach to C#7alt
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Modal Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII augmented - third mode of D melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: F# Lydian Augmented shares the parent scale. Maximum brightness contrast
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Bright augmented major within melodic minor system
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F# maj7#5 (bIII) for bright contrast to dark Super Locrian
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: IV dominant - fourth mode of D melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: G# Lydian Dominant is the signature melodic minor dominant. Both are essential melodic minor dominant modes
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion vs bebop. Lydian Dominant for #11 fusion sound, Super Locrian for complete alteration
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#7#11 (IV7) for bright fusion dominant vs C#7alt dark altered dominant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: V dominant - fifth mode of D melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: A Mixolydian b6 provides dark dominant. Shares melodic minor parent
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion. Dark sophisticated dominant typical of melodic minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A7 (V7) for dark dominant within D melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, World Music, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: VI half-diminished - sixth mode of D melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: B Aeolian b5 is the essential half-diminished scale. Shares melodic minor parent
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary. Go-to half-diminished scale
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bm7♭5 (VI) in D melodic minor context
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Modal Jazz, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Bebop scale combining altered and natural tensions
compatibleScales.rationale: Bebop dominant adds chromatic passing tone for eighth-note runs. Combines with Super Locrian for complete bebop vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz. Essential for Charlie Parker style lines
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use bebop dominant for chromatic bebop runs. Super Locrian for sustained altered tones
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Contemporary Jazz, Hard Bop

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical scale over altered dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone provides completely ambiguous altered dominant. Contains #4/#11 and shares suspended quality
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist, jazz, avant-garde. Maximum ambiguity over altered dominants
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole tone for completely suspended sound. Super Locrian for bebop-based alteration
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Contemporary Classical, Film Score, Ambient

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent diatonic scale - pentatonic uses scale degrees 1, 2, 3, 5, 6
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Major Pentatonic is a subset of C# major, containing only the most consonant notes (C#, D#, E#, G#, A#). Avoids the 4th and 7th which create tension, making it impossible to play a 'wrong' note
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres - rock, blues, country, pop, folk, world music. The most user-friendly scale for improvisation, creating bright, optimistic, singable melodies
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for improvisation with guaranteed consonance. Works over I-IV-V progressions in C# major. Ideal for creating memorable hooks, vocal melodies, and guitar solos. The safest choice for any major key improvisation
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Folk, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: Works over both - pentatonic avoids the 4th
compatibleScales.rationale: Since pentatonic avoids the 4th degree, it works over both Ionian and Lydian. The absence of the 4th means the #4/#11 characteristic of Lydian doesn't conflict
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, film scores. Major pentatonic provides safe notes over Lydian's bright maj7#11 chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# maj7#11 chords for safe, consonant improvisation. The pentatonic framework prevents clashing with Lydian's #4
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: Works over dominant 7th - pentatonic avoids the 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Major pentatonic works perfectly over C#7 chords because it avoids the b7 (B natural). Provides bright major sound over dominant harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk, country. Major pentatonic over dominant 7th chords is extremely common in rock/country guitar solos
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 chords for bright, major-sounding dominant improvisation. Common in Southern rock, country, and blues-rock styles
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative minor pentatonic - shares all the same notes
compatibleScales.rationale: C# major pentatonic and A# minor pentatonic are the same five notes (C#, D#, E#, G#, A#), just with different emphasis. This relative relationship is fundamental to pentatonic theory
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal. Understanding the major/minor pentatonic relationship allows you to use the same pattern in both major and minor contexts
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think A# minor pentatonic (relative minor) over C# major contexts. The same fingering works for both - just emphasize different root notes
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Alternative, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: IV major - C# pentatonic works as V pentatonic in F# major
compatibleScales.rationale: C# major pentatonic functions beautifully over F# major harmony (IV). Creates bright V-I feeling with pentatonic simplicity
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Country, folk, rock. The I-IV-V pentatonic relationships are fundamental to these genres
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# major pentatonic over F# major (IV) and C# major (I) chords for cohesive pentatonic-based progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Folk, Country, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: V major - pentatonic built from the dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: C# is the V of F# major. C# major pentatonic creates natural dominant-to-tonic movement over V-I progressions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres with V-I cadences. Essential for understanding pentatonic function in Western harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G# major (V) chords leading to C# major (I). Creates smooth pentatonic-based voice leading
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Country, Singer-Songwriter, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic with added b5 blue note
compatibleScales.rationale: Understanding major pentatonic vs blues scale reveals the major/minor pentatonic dichotomy. Blues scale adds minor character with b3 and b5
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock. Major pentatonic for bright sound, blues scale for gritty minor flavor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Alternate between major pentatonic (bright) and blues scale (gritty) over C#7 or C# vamps for dynamic contrast
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Funk

compatibleScales.relationship: III major - built from the major 3rd
compatibleScales.rationale: C# major pentatonic shares most notes with E# major. The III-I relationship is common in contemporary music
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Pop, rock, contemporary. The I-III progression uses major pentatonic smoothly over both chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# major pentatonic over E# major (III) for smooth melodic connection
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Alternative, Indie, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: vi modal scale - pentatonic built from submediant
compatibleScales.rationale: A# is the vi in C# major. The pentatonic notes work beautifully over Dorian's minor tonality
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, fusion. Pentatonic simplicity over Dorian sophistication
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# major pentatonic over A#m7 (vi) for pentatonic-based minor improvisation
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Gospel

compatibleScales.relationship: ii minor - pentatonic over supertonic
compatibleScales.rationale: C# major pentatonic works over D#m (ii) chords, common in ii-V-I progressions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, pop, R&B. Pentatonic approach to ii-V-I changes
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D#m7 (ii) in ii-V-I progressions for pentatonic-based jazz improvisation
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Folk, Alternative, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent diatonic scale - pentatonic uses scale degrees 1, b3, 4, 5, b7
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Minor Pentatonic is a subset of C# natural minor (C#, E, F#, G#, B), containing only the safest minor notes. Avoids the 2nd and 6th which can create tension, making it the most reliable minor scale for improvisation
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal minor sound - rock, blues, metal, funk, pop, world music. THE fundamental scale for rock/blues guitar solos. The most commonly used scale in rock music history
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for minor improvisation with guaranteed consonance. Works over C#m, C#m7, and i-iv-v progressions. Essential for blues-rock, metal riffs, and funk grooves. The safest minor improvisation choice
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Blues, Grunge, Alternative

compatibleScales.relationship: Works perfectly - pentatonic avoids the 6th
compatibleScales.rationale: Since minor pentatonic avoids the 6th degree, it works over both Aeolian (b6) and Dorian (natural 6). The absence of the 6th means Dorian's characteristic major 6th doesn't conflict
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, fusion. Minor pentatonic provides safe framework over Dorian's sophisticated jazz minor sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7 (Dorian) for safe, bluesy improvisation. Pentatonic simplicity works perfectly over Dorian's jazz complexity
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Compatible - pentatonic avoids the b2
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic works over Phrygian because it avoids the characteristic b2. Provides accessible minor sound over Spanish/flamenco Phrygian harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Metal, Spanish, Middle Eastern. Minor pentatonic offers familiar sound over exotic Phrygian contexts
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# Phrygian for accessible minor riffs. Common in metal for combining pentatonic simplicity with Phrygian darkness
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Thrash Metal, Death Metal, Progressive Metal

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative major pentatonic - shares all the same notes
compatibleScales.rationale: C# minor pentatonic and E major pentatonic are the same five notes (C#, E, F#, G#, B), just with different root emphasis. This relative relationship is fundamental
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal. The major/minor pentatonic relationship allows the same scale pattern to work in both major and minor contexts
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Think E major pentatonic (relative major) over C# minor contexts. Same fingering, different root emphasis. Essential concept for all pentatonic playing
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Blues, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic with added b5 blue note
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale adds the characteristic b5 (G natural) to minor pentatonic. The blue note creates the definitive blues/rock sound while maintaining pentatonic simplicity
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz-blues. The blues scale is THE signature sound of blues and rock guitar from B.B. King to Jimi Hendrix to Slash
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Add the G natural (b5) blue note to minor pentatonic for authentic blues sound. The b5 creates tension resolving to the perfect 5th - the essence of blues
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Funk

compatibleScales.relationship: iv minor - pentatonic built from subdominant
compatibleScales.rationale: C# minor pentatonic works beautifully over F#m (iv) chords. The i-iv-v pentatonic relationships are fundamental to blues and rock
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, metal. The i-iv progression with minor pentatonic is essential to these genres
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# minor pentatonic over F#m (iv) and C#m (i) for cohesive pentatonic-based minor progressions. Essential for blues/rock rhythm changes
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Folk, Gothic, Dark Wave

compatibleScales.relationship: v minor - pentatonic over dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic works over v minor chords (G#m). In minor keys, the v chord is often minor rather than dominant
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, metal, modal minor. The i-v minor progression uses pentatonic smoothly
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# minor pentatonic over G#m (v) for minor-based progressions without dominant function
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Metal, Alternative, Post-Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - common in minor keys
compatibleScales.rationale: C# minor pentatonic shares most notes with E major. The i-bIII progression is extremely common in rock and pop
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, pop, metal. The i-bIII relationship provides major relief in minor contexts
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# minor pentatonic over E major (bIII) for smooth melodic connection. Very common in contemporary rock
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Indie, Alternative, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: bVI major - provides major contrast
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic works over bVI major chords, common in minor key progressions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, pop. The i-bVI progression uses pentatonic for melodic continuity
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over A major (bVI) for bright major moments in minor key contexts
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Alternative, Indie, Emo

compatibleScales.relationship: Compatible - pentatonic avoids the major 7th
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic works over harmonic minor because it avoids the raised 7th. Provides simple minor sound over complex harmonic minor contexts
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, metal, flamenco. Pentatonic simplicity over harmonic minor sophistication
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use minor pentatonic for accessible riffs over harmonic minor progressions. Common in neoclassical metal
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Metal, Neoclassical, Film Score, Gothic

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent scale - blues scale adds the b5 blue note (G natural) to minor pentatonic
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Blues Scale is C# minor pentatonic (C#, E, F#, G#, B) with added b5 (G natural). The blue note creates the characteristic blues sound - the defining element that transforms pentatonic into blues
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz-blues, funk. THE signature scale of blues guitar from Robert Johnson to B.B. King to Stevie Ray Vaughan to modern blues-rock. The most important scale in blues music history
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for blues and rock improvisation. The G natural (b5) is the 'blue note' - use it as a chromatic passing tone or bend into it from F# for authentic blues sound. Works over C#7, C#m7, and i-iv-v blues progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues scale works perfectly over dominant 7th chords
compatibleScales.rationale: The blues scale's combination of minor 3rd (E) and minor 7th (B) creates the classic blues sound over dominant 7th chords. The b5 (G) adds characteristic blues tension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz-blues. The blues scale over dominant 7th chords is fundamental to blues harmony. Creates the signature blues ambiguity between major and minor
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 chords for authentic blues sound. The minor 3rd (E) against the implied major 3rd (E#) of C#7 creates the distinctive blues 'blue note' tension
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Country Blues, Jazz Blues

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues scale provides bluesy alternative to Mixolydian
compatibleScales.rationale: Mixolydian is the diatonic dominant scale; blues scale provides the gritty, minor-inflected blues alternative. Both work over C#7, but with very different character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues-rock. Mixolydian for major-sounding rock solos, blues scale for grittier blues-rock tone
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Alternate between Mixolydian (major sound) and blues scale (minor/blues sound) over C#7 for dynamic contrast in rock/blues solos
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: iv blues - blues scale over subdominant
compatibleScales.rationale: The I7-IV7 progression (C#7-F#7) is THE fundamental blues progression. Using blues scale over both chords is the essence of 12-bar blues
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, blues-rock, jazz-blues. The 12-bar blues form uses I7-IV7-V7 with blues scale over all three chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# blues scale over C#7 (I7) and F#7 (IV7) for authentic 12-bar blues. The same scale works over both chords - fundamental to blues playing
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Country Blues, Southern Rock, Blues Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: V blues - blues scale over dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: In 12-bar blues, the V7 chord (G#7) also uses the blues scale. The I7-IV7-V7 progression uses blues scale over all three chords
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, blues-rock. Essential for understanding 12-bar blues structure
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# blues scale over G#7 (V7) in blues progressions. The flexibility of blues scale over I7-IV7-V7 is what makes blues improvisation accessible
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Southern Rock, Jazz Blues

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative major pentatonic contrast
compatibleScales.rationale: E major pentatonic (relative major) provides bright major contrast to the blues scale's minor character. Alternating between them creates major/minor dynamic
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, country. Using both major and minor pentatonic/blues creates the characteristic blues sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Alternate between C# blues scale (minor/gritty) and E major pentatonic (bright/major) for dynamic blues solos
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Blues, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues scale works over natural minor harmony
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale provides bluesy approach to minor harmony. The b5 adds blues character to standard minor progressions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues-rock, rock, metal. Blues scale over minor chords creates blues-influenced minor sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use blues scale over C#m or C#m7 for bluesy minor sound. More accessible than full natural minor scale
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Blues, Grunge, Alternative

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues scale provides bluesy alternative to Dorian
compatibleScales.rationale: Dorian is the jazz minor scale; blues scale provides the blues/rock alternative. Both work over C#m7, different character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz-blues, funk, fusion. Blues scale for blues flavor, Dorian for jazz sophistication
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use blues scale for blues/rock minor sound. Use Dorian for jazz/funk minor sound. Both work over C#m7
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Both contain b5 - different functions
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale's b5 (G) relates to diminished harmony. Diminished scale provides more chromatic approach to similar tones
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz-blues, bebop. Understanding the b5 connection helps integrate diminished concepts into blues
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Explore diminished scale for chromatic blues lines. Both scales share the important b5 tension note
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Bebop, Free Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Major vs minor pentatonic contrast
compatibleScales.rationale: Major pentatonic provides bright contrast to blues scale's minor character. Understanding both creates complete pentatonic vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Country, rock, blues. Alternating major and minor pentatonic creates dynamic melodic variety
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use major pentatonic for bright moments, blues scale for minor/gritty moments. Essential contrast in country and rock
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Blues, Folk

compatibleScales.relationship: Subset of Phrygian - emphasizes b2 characteristic
compatibleScales.rationale: Japanese pentatonic (In Sen scale) contains the characteristic b2 (D) of Phrygian plus minor 3rd, perfect 5th, and minor 7th. Creates dark, exotic pentatonic sound reminiscent of traditional Japanese music
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Japanese traditional music, world fusion, film scores, video game music, ambient. The In Sen scale evokes Japanese koto and shamisen music. Used extensively in Asian-themed compositions and meditation music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m or C# Phrygian harmony for authentic Japanese/Asian sound. The b2 (D) creates characteristic Eastern tension. Perfect for film scores, world music, and creating exotic Eastern atmosphere
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: World, Japanese Traditional, Anime, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Related pentatonic with different character
compatibleScales.rationale: Standard minor pentatonic uses major 2nd (D#) and perfect 4th (F#), while Japanese pentatonic uses b2 (D) and perfect 4th (F#). Both are minor pentatonics but with completely different emotional character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues/rock vs Japanese/Asian. Minor pentatonic for Western blues, Japanese pentatonic for Eastern exoticism
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use minor pentatonic for blues/rock. Use Japanese pentatonic for Asian/Eastern flavor. Understanding the difference expands pentatonic vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Shares b2 characteristic - dominant version
compatibleScales.rationale: Phrygian Dominant also features the b2 creating Spanish/Middle Eastern sound. Japanese pentatonic provides simpler, pentatonic approach to similar exotic b2 harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Spanish flamenco vs Japanese traditional. Both use b2 for exotic character but different cultural contexts
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7 with b2 emphasis for exotic dominant. Japanese pentatonic for Eastern, Phrygian Dominant for Spanish flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Spanish Music, Gypsy Jazz, Latin Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Shares dark minor character with b2
compatibleScales.rationale: Locrian contains the b2 and b5. Japanese pentatonic avoids the b5 but maintains the dark b2 character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Diminished/dark contexts. Japanese pentatonic provides accessible pentatonic framework for dark harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Japanese pentatonic over dark, diminished, or suspended minor contexts for exotic Eastern flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Doom Metal, Avant-Garde Metal, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: Built from the b2 - interesting harmonic color
compatibleScales.rationale: D major (the b2 of C#) provides interesting color relationship. Japanese pentatonic can create modal interchange with bII major harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Film scores, contemporary classical, world fusion. Modal mixture using bII creates sophisticated harmonic color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Explore over D major (bII) for exotic harmonic flavor. Creates unique modal interchange possibilities
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, World Fusion, Contemporary, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: iv minor - Japanese pentatonic over subdominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Japanese pentatonic works over F#m (iv) providing exotic minor color in C# contexts
compatibleScales.musicalContext: World music, fusion. Creates Eastern-influenced minor progressions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F#m (iv) for Eastern minor flavor in modal progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Folk, Gothic, Dark Wave

compatibleScales.relationship: v minor - pentatonic over dominant minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Works over G#m (v) for exotic minor v sound instead of dominant function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal minor, world fusion. Creates non-functional modal minor sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#m (v) for Eastern-influenced modal minor progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Metal, Alternative, Post-Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Different pentatonic approaches to minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Blues scale has b5, Japanese pentatonic has b2. Both create exotic pentatonic minor but different characters
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Western blues vs Eastern traditional. Understanding both expands world music vocabulary
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Blues scale for Western blues, Japanese pentatonic for Eastern exoticism
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Funk

compatibleScales.relationship: bIII major - common in minor contexts
compatibleScales.rationale: E major (bIII) provides major relief in minor contexts. Japanese pentatonic can work over this for exotic color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: World fusion, contemporary. Creates interesting harmonic color
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Explore over E major (bIII) for unique melodic color
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Indie, Alternative, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: bVII minor - flat seventh relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Japanese pentatonic can work over Bm (bVII) for modal color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal, world music. Creates non-functional modal relationships
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Bm (bVII) for modal Eastern-influenced sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Folk, Alternative, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: Subset of Dorian - uses 1, 2, 4, 5, b7
compatibleScales.rationale: Egyptian pentatonic (C#, D#, F#, G#, B) is a subset of Dorian, containing root, major 2nd, perfect 4th, perfect 5th, and minor 7th. Creates suspended, modal minor sound reminiscent of Middle Eastern music
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Middle Eastern music, North African, world fusion, film scores, meditation music. Evokes ancient Egyptian, Arabic, and desert themes. Popular in ethnic fusion and film soundtracks
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7 or Dorian contexts for authentic Middle Eastern/Egyptian sound. The absence of the 3rd creates suspended, modal quality. Perfect for film scores, world music, and ethnic fusion
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: World, Middle Eastern, Film Score, Ambient

compatibleScales.relationship: Egyptian pentatonic avoids the 3rd - perfect for sus chords
compatibleScales.rationale: The absence of the 3rd (E or E#) makes Egyptian pentatonic ideal for suspended chords. Creates floating, unresolved harmony characteristic of Middle Eastern music
compatibleScales.musicalContext: World music, ambient, film scores, meditation. Suspended harmony is fundamental to Middle Eastern and Egyptian traditional music
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#sus2, C#sus4, C#m7sus4 for authentic suspended Middle Eastern sound. The lack of 3rd maintains the suspended character
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: World, Ambient, New Age, Meditation

compatibleScales.relationship: Subset of Mixolydian - works over dominant without 3rd
compatibleScales.rationale: Egyptian pentatonic is also a subset of Mixolydian. Works over C#7 chords while avoiding the major 3rd, creating suspended dominant sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal rock, world fusion, jam bands. Suspended dominant sound common in jam band and modal rock
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7sus or C#7 for suspended dominant sound. Creates modal dominant without major/minor commitment
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Southern Rock, Country, Jam Band

compatibleScales.relationship: IV major - Egyptian pentatonic built from subdominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Egyptian pentatonic works beautifully over F# major (IV). Creates suspended quality over IV chord
compatibleScales.musicalContext: World fusion, contemporary. Suspended pentatonic over IV creates interesting harmonic ambiguity
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over F# major (IV) for suspended, modal color in I-IV progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Folk, Country, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: Different pentatonic structure - Egyptian uses 2nd, minor uses b3
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic has b3 (E) giving it clear minor character. Egyptian pentatonic has major 2nd (D#) and no 3rd, creating suspended character
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues/rock vs Middle Eastern. Minor pentatonic for blues, Egyptian for Middle Eastern/modal sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use minor pentatonic for blues/rock minor. Use Egyptian pentatonic for suspended, Middle Eastern, or modal sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Grunge, Hard Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: v minor - Egyptian pentatonic over minor dominant
compatibleScales.rationale: Works over G#m (v) for suspended minor v sound instead of dominant function
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal, world music. Creates non-functional modal minor sound
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over G#m (v) for Middle Eastern-influenced modal progressions
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Metal, Alternative, Post-Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: ii minor - Egyptian pentatonic over supertonic
compatibleScales.rationale: Works over D#m (ii) for suspended ii sound in progressions
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal, jazz-fusion, world. Creates suspended quality in ii-V-I
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over D#m7 (ii) for suspended, modal ii sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Pop, Folk, Alternative, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: bVII major - flat seventh relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: Egyptian pentatonic can work over B major (bVII) for modal color
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal rock, progressive. bVII creates modal, non-functional harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over B major (bVII) for modal rock sound with Middle Eastern flavor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Singer-Songwriter, Indie

compatibleScales.relationship: Egyptian pentatonic avoids the 6th - works over minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Works over natural minor by avoiding the b6. Creates suspended minor sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Modal minor, world music. Suspended minor quality over traditional minor harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m for suspended, modal minor sound without committing to b6 or natural 6
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Blues, Grunge, Alternative

compatibleScales.relationship: Different exotic pentatonic - Japanese has b2, Egyptian has major 2nd
compatibleScales.rationale: Both are exotic pentatonics but different characters. Japanese has dark b2, Egyptian has brighter major 2nd and suspended quality
compatibleScales.musicalContext: East Asian vs Middle Eastern. Understanding both expands world music pentatonic vocabulary
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Japanese pentatonic for Eastern Asia, Egyptian for Middle Eastern/North African sound
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: World, Japanese Traditional, Anime, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Contains all 12 pitches - works over everything
compatibleScales.rationale: The chromatic scale contains all 12 notes of Western music (C#, D, D#, E, E#, F#, F##/G, G#, A, A#, B, B#). It's the master set from which all other scales derive. Works over any chord, any key, any harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres - classical, jazz, bebop, contemporary classical, avant-garde. Essential for chromatic passing tones, bebop lines, modern jazz, and connecting diatonic scales smoothly
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic notes as passing tones between chord tones and scale degrees. Essential for bebop eighth-note runs, smooth voice leading, and connecting any two notes. The foundation of chromatic harmony and voice leading
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: All Genres, Universal, Jazz, Classical

compatibleScales.relationship: Bebop scales use chromatic passing tones
compatibleScales.rationale: Bebop scales (dominant, major, minor, dorian) add chromatic passing tones to diatonic scales. Understanding chromatic scale is essential for bebop improvisation
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, jazz. Chromatic passing tones allow chord tones to fall on downbeats in bebop eighth-note runs
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic passing tones to connect scale degrees smoothly. Essential technique in Charlie Parker, Dizzy Gillespie, and all bebop playing
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Contemporary Jazz, Hard Bop

compatibleScales.relationship: Blues scale includes chromatic blue note
compatibleScales.rationale: The blue note (b5) is a chromatic note added to minor pentatonic. Chromatic notes create the essential blues sound
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz-blues. Chromatic approach notes and passing tones define blues guitar vocabulary
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic approach tones in blues. Approach any target note from a half-step above or below
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Funk

compatibleScales.relationship: Altered scale contains many chromatic alterations
compatibleScales.rationale: Super Locrian has b9, #9, #11, b13 - all chromatic alterations. Understanding chromatic scale helps navigate altered dominants
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, bebop, fusion. Altered dominants use chromatic notes extensively
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic approach to altered tones. Connect altered scale notes with chromatic passing tones for smooth bebop lines
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Bebop, Post-Bop, Contemporary Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Diminished scales use chromatic intervals
compatibleScales.rationale: Diminished scales alternate half and whole steps, using 8 of the 12 chromatic notes. Understanding chromatic scale reveals diminished structure
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, classical, contemporary. Diminished scales provide chromatic approach to dominant and diminished chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic passing tones between diminished scale notes for smooth lines
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Bebop, Free Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Chromatic notes fill gaps between major scale degrees
compatibleScales.rationale: C# major has 7 notes; chromatic scale adds the 5 'in-between' notes. These chromatic passing tones connect major scale degrees smoothly
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres. Chromatic passing tones make melodies smoother and more sophisticated
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic notes to connect major scale degrees. Approach any major scale note from a half-step above or below
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Pop, Rock, Country, Folk, Singer-Songwriter

compatibleScales.relationship: Chromatic notes fill gaps between minor scale degrees
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor has 7 notes; chromatic provides the 5 chromatic passing tones. Essential for smooth minor key voice leading
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres with minor tonality. Chromatic passing tones are fundamental to classical and jazz minor harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic approach notes in minor contexts. Essential for smooth melodic lines in minor keys
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Blues, Grunge, Alternative

compatibleScales.relationship: Chromatic connection between harmonic and melodic minor
compatibleScales.rationale: Chromatic notes connect different minor scales. Understanding chromatic motion between minor variants is essential for jazz
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, classical. Chromatic voice leading between minor scale types
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic notes to transition smoothly between natural, harmonic, and melodic minor
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Chromatic passing tones enhance Dorian improvisation
compatibleScales.rationale: Dorian is the jazz minor scale; chromatic passing tones are essential for jazz vocabulary
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, fusion. Chromatic bebop lines over Dorian vamps
