compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic approach notes for sophisticated Dorian improvisation. Essential for Miles Davis/Herbie Hancock style lines
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Chromatic passing tones connect any changes smoothly
compatibleScales.rationale: Chromatic scale provides the smoothest possible voice leading between any two chords. Essential for chord melody, arranging, and composition
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, classical, contemporary. Chromatic voice leading is fundamental to all advanced harmony
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use chromatic notes to connect chord tones smoothly in any progression. The key to smooth voice leading and sophisticated harmony
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: All Genres, Jazz, Classical, Contemporary

compatibleScales.relationship: Whole tone scale contains C# augmented triad and creates augmented harmony
compatibleScales.rationale: Whole tone scale (C#, D#, E#, F##, G##, A##) is built from six notes spaced a whole step apart. Contains the C# augmented triad (C#-E#-G##) and creates completely ambiguous, floating, suspended augmented harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist classical (Debussy, Ravel), jazz, film scores, avant-garde, video game music. The signature sound of French impressionism. Creates dreamlike, floating, unresolved quality
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C# aug, C#7#5, or C# maj7#5 chords for impressionist suspended sound. Perfect for film scores, jazz reharmonization, and creating ambiguous harmonic suspension. No avoid notes - every note works
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Jazz, Film Score, Contemporary Classical

compatibleScales.relationship: Shares augmented 5th - both create bright suspended harmony
compatibleScales.rationale: Lydian Augmented (melodic minor mode) has augmented 5th and #4. Whole tone provides completely symmetrical augmented alternative with maximum suspension
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz vs impressionist. Lydian Augmented for diatonic melodic minor, whole tone for complete symmetrical ambiguity
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Lydian Augmented for diatonic melodic approach. Use whole tone for completely suspended impressionist sound. Both work over maj7#5
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Film Score

compatibleScales.relationship: Both symmetrical augmented scales
compatibleScales.rationale: Augmented scale (hexatonic) alternates minor 3rd and half step. Whole tone uses only whole steps. Both create augmented harmony but different symmetrical patterns
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Both provide symmetrical approach to augmented chords
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use whole tone for impressionist floating sound. Use augmented scale for chromatic jazz augmented approach. Different flavors of augmented
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: There are only two whole tone scales in music
compatibleScales.rationale: Only two distinct whole tone scales exist in 12-tone music. C# whole tone and D whole tone (a half-step apart). F## whole tone = C# whole tone (same notes, enharmonically)
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist, jazz. Understanding this symmetry is fundamental to whole tone theory
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: C# and F## whole tone are the same scale. This symmetry means patterns repeat every major 3rd (4 frets on guitar)
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Jazz, Film Score, Contemporary Classical

compatibleScales.relationship: The 'other' whole tone scale - every note is a whole step different
compatibleScales.rationale: D# whole tone is the only other distinct whole tone scale. Together, C# and D# whole tone scales contain all 12 chromatic notes
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist, jazz. The two whole tone scales divide the octave symmetrically
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# whole tone over C# aug chords. Use D# whole tone over D# aug chords. They never overlap
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Jazz, Film Score, Contemporary Classical

compatibleScales.relationship: Whole tone scale works perfectly over augmented dominant 7th chords
compatibleScales.rationale: C#7#5 chord (C#-E#-G##-B) sits within whole tone scale. Creates floating dominant with #5 and no clear resolution
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, impressionist, film scores. Augmented dominant for suspended tension without traditional resolution
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#7#5 or C#7alt for suspended dominant. Creates impressionist unresolved dominant color
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Avant-Garde, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Shares #4 brightness - both create suspended major harmony
compatibleScales.rationale: Lydian has #4, whole tone has #4 (#11). Both create bright suspended major sound, but whole tone is completely ambiguous
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion vs impressionist. Lydian for functional bright major, whole tone for suspended ambiguity
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Lydian for bright functional major. Use whole tone for completely suspended impressionist major
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Score, Fusion, Progressive Rock, Dream Pop

compatibleScales.relationship: Both work over dominant 7th with #11
compatibleScales.rationale: Lydian Dominant (melodic minor mode) has b7 and #4. Whole tone also works over 7#11 but with complete ambiguity
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz fusion vs impressionist. Lydian Dominant for functional fusion dominant, whole tone for suspended ambiguous dominant
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Lydian Dominant for jazz fusion 7#11 sound. Use whole tone for impressionist suspended dominant
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz Fusion, Contemporary Jazz, Progressive Rock, Instrumental

compatibleScales.relationship: Same whole tone scale - symmetry repeats every major 3rd
compatibleScales.rationale: G## (enharmonically A) whole tone = C# whole tone. The symmetry means the scale repeats every major 3rd
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Impressionist, jazz. Understanding symmetrical repetition enables pattern-based improvisation
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Same patterns work every major 3rd up/down. This symmetry simplifies whole tone improvisation
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Impressionist, Jazz, Film Score, Contemporary Classical

compatibleScales.relationship: Whole tone contains three augmented triads
compatibleScales.rationale: C# whole tone contains C# aug, E# aug, and G## aug (all the same chord, enharmonically). Works over all three
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, impressionist. Augmented triads' symmetry connects directly to whole tone's symmetry
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use C# whole tone over C# aug, E# aug, G## aug, or A aug (enharmonic) - they're all the same chord
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Impressionist, Contemporary Classical, Avant-Garde

compatibleScales.relationship: Half-whole diminished is THE scale for diminished 7th chords
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole diminished (C#, D, E, F, G, G#, A#, B) alternates half and whole steps, creating an 8-note symmetrical scale. Contains C#dim7 chord (C#-E-G-A#) and works perfectly over all diminished 7th harmony
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, jazz, bebop, contemporary classical. Essential for diminished chord passages, passing diminished chords, and symmetrical harmonic movement. Used extensively by Coltrane, Parker, and classical composers
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#dim7 or C#°7 chords for complete symmetrical diminished sound. Every other note forms the diminished 7th chord. Essential for jazz diminished passages and classical diminished harmony
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Classical, Contemporary Classical, Chamber Music

compatibleScales.relationship: Half-whole diminished works over half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.rationale: Half-whole diminished provides symmetrical approach to half-diminished (C#m7♭5) harmony. More chromatic than Locrian #2 or Aeolian ♭5
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, contemporary classical. Symmetrical alternative to diatonic half-diminished scales
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7♭5 for symmetrical, chromatic half-diminished sound. Alternative to Locrian #2 or Aeolian ♭5
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Contemporary Jazz, Bebop, Free Jazz

compatibleScales.relationship: Symmetrical - the scale repeats every minor 3rd
compatibleScales.rationale: Due to symmetry, C# dim scale = E dim scale = G dim scale = Bb dim scale. Only three distinct diminished scales exist in 12-tone music
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, classical. Understanding this symmetry is fundamental - patterns repeat every 3 frets (minor 3rd)
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Same scale works over C#dim7, Edim7, Gdim7, and Bbdim7. This symmetry simplifies diminished improvisation dramatically
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Classical, Contemporary Classical, Chamber Music

compatibleScales.relationship: Both work over diminished harmony - different approaches
compatibleScales.rationale: Locrian provides diatonic diminished sound from major scale. Half-whole diminished provides symmetrical chromatic approach
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical/modal vs jazz. Locrian for diatonic contexts, half-whole diminished for jazz chromatic approach
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use Locrian for diatonic diminished sound. Use half-whole diminished for symmetrical chromatic diminished
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Metal, Doom Metal, Avant-Garde Metal, Experimental

compatibleScales.relationship: Both work over half-diminished chords
compatibleScales.rationale: Aeolian ♭5 (melodic minor mode) is the diatonic half-diminished scale. Half-whole diminished provides symmetrical chromatic alternative
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz. Aeolian ♭5 for melodic minor contexts, half-whole diminished for symmetrical approach

compatibleScales.relationship: Bebop Dorian is Dorian with added major 3rd (E#) passing tone
compatibleScales.rationale: Bebop Dorian (C#, D#, E, E#, F#, G#, A#, B) adds E# between E (minor 3rd) and F# to create an 8-note scale. Combines Dorian's versatile jazz minor sound with bebop's rhythmic functionality for proper chord tone placement on strong beats over minor 7th chords
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, straight-ahead jazz, hard bop, funk. THE fundamental minor scale for bebop improvisation over minor 7th chords. Used by Charlie Parker, John Coltrane, Miles Davis, and all jazz masters. The go-to bebop scale for ii chords in ii-V-I progressions
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for bebop eighth-note runs over C#m7 chords. The added E# creates proper rhythmic placement of chord tones on downbeats and provides smooth chromatic motion between minor and major 3rd. Perfect for ii chord improvisation, minor vamps, and modal Dorian contexts
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Soul, R&B, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: Bebop Dorian outlines C#m7 chord perfectly
compatibleScales.rationale: The 8-note structure ensures C#m7 chord tones (root, b3, 5, b7) land on downbeats. The major 3rd passing tone creates chromatic voice leading characteristic of bebop
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Bebop, jazz, funk. Bebop Dorian is THE scale for minor 7th chords in jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over C#m7 for authentic bebop Dorian phrasing. Essential for ii chord in ii-V-I and all Dorian modal contexts
